missionStats: 16 of 18 completed; 0 quit early. Failed (2): f02 (Frank, non-technical retiree) — eat-healthy and steps checklist answers never registered (c16) and food logging was impossible without an API key, leaving his day only partly logged; s06 (Omar) — mission was switching to a bulk, which the cut-only goal engine makes impossible (c02), and he lost his existing plan to the unconfirmed 'End plan' (c17) while trying to work around it. Caveat: many 'completed' missions succeeded only via workarounds — weight logged by editing the phantom Trends/History entry, food reduced to the binary Yes/No toggle, s02's workout logged via History backfill after the dashboard Done button failed.
clusterNotes: Clean cohort split: fresh installs (f01-f11) exclusively hit the onboarding/seed-data/goal-sheet clusters (c03, c07, c08, c09 are all-f), while established accounts (s01-s07) exclusively hit the Trends contradiction, targets-as-actuals, and ladder-framing clusters (c12, c21, c24 are all-s); the silent-tap cluster (c11) skews s-cohort. All 18 reports mention the Noof greeting (c01). The three least technical personas (f02, f10, s03) absorbed the worst of the jargon and API-key walls, and the only fresh-cohort mission failure was the retiree; all three performance users (f07, f08, f09) churned over binary-only logging (c14). Likely shared root causes worth engineering attention: f08's hidden-duplicate-input finding probably explains every typed-value revert in c08, and lingering sheets/toasts (c05, c22) plausibly cause the swallowed first taps in c11 — f05 explicitly observed the stuck sheet eating taps. Judgment calls/folds: s04's misrouted 'Add API key' button folded into c10; s05's plain-language privacy-statement request folded into c06; s06's stale cut validation and Fuel's permanent 'Cut targets' folded into c02; f09's performance-goal request folded into c02; f02's '1 to go' ring arithmetic folded into c19 (may be a distinct counter bug); f04's first-tap Done placed under c11 rather than c05 — could belong to either. Ambiguous: the phantom seed entry's date varied across testers (Jul 15 vs Jul 16), suggesting a timezone-dependent seeding bug (noted under c25). Not schematized but notable: 7 explicit home-screen deletes, all citing 'built for exactly one user' plus credential walls; ~6 provisional keeps, all crediting the sub-minute one-tap logging and local-first/no-account privacy — the two most-praised traits across otherwise negative reports. All consoleErrors arrays were empty; nothing else was dropped.

cluster.title: App is hardcoded to Noof — no onboarding, no profile, everyone greeted by someone else's name
cluster.description: Every tester was greeted 'Morning/Evening, Noof' atop Noof's Push/Pull/Legs rotation, 5/week quota, office meal schedule, and 'built for exactly one user. LFG.' About copy. There is no first-run setup and no name/profile setting anywhere. Fresh-install users (f01-f11) thought they'd opened someone else's account; established users with profile names (s-cohort) still got 'Noof'. The single most-cited reason for deleting the app.
quotes: Frank: "It feels like I moved into somebody else's house — his name's on the mailbox, his lunches are in the fridge"
f01: "bestie, that user is not me, so it's cute but it's getting deleted"
Ruth: "after six weeks it still calls me Noof"

LFG.'); js/app.js:1216-1295 (More tab has no name/profile field); js/store.js:26 (profile name hardcoded 'Noof', birthdate '2000-09-23'); js/coach.js:117,159,177 (coach prompts hardcode 'Dylan ("Noof"), 25, male'); manifest.webmanifest:4 ('Built for Noof.')

There is no onboarding, and the More tab exposes no name setting. Coach/food AI prompts also hardcode Dylan's identity. cluster.title: Goal engine is cut-only — no bulk, maintain, or performance goals; targets above current average rejected
cluster.description: Any target above the stored average is rejected ('Cutting — target must be below your 165.0/173.0 lb average') and there is no gain/maintain toggle or performance goal type. s06's validation persisted even after ending his plan, a fresh goal sheet defaulted to a deeper cut, and Fuel permanently shows 'Cut targets' (f03 also noted cutting defaults on a fresh install). Hard mission failure for s06 (bulk to 180), a wall for f08 (bulk to 205), and f09's marathon training had nothing to select.
quotes: Omar: "I finished my cut, tried to tell it I'm bulking to 180, and it flat-out refused"
f08: "I wanted to bulk to 205 and it refused: 'Cutting — target must be below your 165.0 lb average.'"
f09: "no race, pace, mileage, or any performance goal exists"

votes.evidence: js/app.js:927-930, 975-977 (Cut/Bulk toggle in Trends); js/app.js:1149-1150 (bulk validation exists, inverted); js/app.js:351 (Fuel label flips to 'Bulk targets'); js/app.js:1130-1160 (goal sheet has no mode control, error omits the toggle); js/store.js:39,206 (default 'cut'); js/plan.js:64-66 (clearGoal never resets mode); js/app.js:1140 (default = avg−7); js/plan.js:20 + js/store.js:41 (no maintain or performance goal types)

Testers missed this mitigation because the goal sheet has no mode control and its rejection message never points to the toggle — mode defaults to cut, survives ending a plan, and defaults suggest a deeper cut, so the trap they describe is real. Maintain and performance goals genuinely don't exist.

votes.evidence: app.js:927-930, app.js:975-977, app.js:1132, app.js:1140, app.js:1149-1150, app.js:1157-1160, app.js:351; plan.js:20, plan.js:64-66; store.js:39, store.js:206; fuel.js:30; charts.js:60; coach.js:78

cluster.title: Food logging walled behind a bring-your-own Anthropic API key — no manual fallback

Non-technical users didn't know what an API key is, f10 refused on trust grounds (unclear what data goes where), and s03's six already-logged food days are also invisible without the key.

f05: "Food logging wants me to buy an API key from a developer console — I have a five-month-old."
Frank: "there was no simple place to write down my oatmeal"

A key-free manual path exists (hand-build, search-add, item edit sheets, all local Store writes), but it renders only past the key gate, so keyless users cannot reach it; the "Logged days" history is likewise hidden, confirming s03. Only the binary ateHealthy toggle on Today is ungated. Intentional gating, but the wall and surprise are real.

votes.evidence: js/app.js:462-471 (keyless gate renders pitch card + early return); js/app.js:509,563-571 (manual "build the list by hand" only in keyed branch); js/app.js:513-532 (Logged days history behind gate); js/food.js:128-129 (parse throws 'no-key'); food.js:184 sole non-manual Store.setFood caller

cluster.title: Bottom sheets can't be dismissed: no X/Cancel, backdrop taps ignored, sheets linger and stack across tabs
cluster.screen: Set your goal sheet, weight/day-editor sheets (persist over all tabs)
cluster.description: Sheets have no close affordance, don't respond to backdrop/Escape, and often stay open after the primary action. f03 and f08 had to force-reload the app to escape the goal sheet; f06 was cornered into committing a plan he knew was wrong just to exit; f05's stuck sheet swallowed taps for the whole session; f02 ended up with three number inputs live at once so typing landed in the wrong boxes; s03 fought a day-editor window across three tabs.
quotes: f08: "trapped me in the sheet like a hostage negotiation"
Tom: "the only way out I could find was committing a plan I knew was based on a wrong weight"
Ruth: "fought the window across three tabs before it closed"

votes.evidence: js/app.js:28-37 (openSheet replaces #sheet innerHTML; backdrop click → closeSheet), css/app.css:655-659 (open backdrop inset:0, pointer-events:auto, z-90) vs css/app.css:127 (tabbar z-50), js/app.js:816-820,832 (day sheet's three number inputs + Done), js/app.js:1171-1172 (goal sheet's only buttons commit/end), js/app.js:1103 (sole keydown handler — no Escape), grep touchstart|pointerdown|drag = 0 matches (grab handle decorative)
votes.explanation: Backdrop tap closes every sheet (app.js:37; pointer-events:auto, covers the z-50 tab bar), and openSheet replaces innerHTML, so "ignored backdrop taps," stacked sheets, cross-tab persistence, and forced reloads cannot occur as described. f02's "three inputs" are the one day-sheet's own steps/weight/BF fields. Real gaps testers hit: no X/Cancel on any sheet, goal sheet's only visible button commits, no Escape handler, grab handle implies a swipe that doesn't exist, day editor stays open until Done. Discoverability problem, not a missing dismiss.

votes.evidence: js/app.js:29-37 (backdrop click closes; innerHTML replaced, no stacking); css/app.css:655-659 (full-screen backdrop, pointer-events:auto); css/app.css:127 vs 657 (tabbar z-50 under backdrop z-90 — tabs unreachable while sheet open); js/app.js:854,1124,1204 (primary actions call closeSheet); css/app.css:676 + no touch handlers in js/ (grab handle decorative); no Escape keydown anywhere in js/
votes.explanation: Mechanics are misdescribed: every sheet closes on a backdrop tap (app.js:37; backdrop is full-screen with pointer-events:auto); openSheet replaces #sheet innerHTML so stacking is impossible; the backdrop covers the tab bar, so sheets cannot persist across tabs; and all primary actions call closeSheet. The real kernel: no X/Cancel button, no Escape handler, and a grab handle implying a swipe-dismiss that doesn't exist — a genuine discoverability gap, but with a working one-tap escape testers missed.

cluster.title: Sync token is an unexplained dead end — reminders/auto-sync impossible, and no plain statement of what leaves the phone
cluster.description: The bare 'sync token' field gates the 9pm reminder push, auto-steps, and auto weigh-ins via 'your private worker', with no link, definition, or acquisition path — so no tester could enable reminders. Privacy-minded users (f03, f11, s05, s07) read the mystery box as a trust wobble because nothing says what server it is or what data flows to it; s05 had to assemble the app's local-first story from three separate screens and asked for one plain-language privacy sentence.

The exact plain-language data-flow sentence exists only as a sync.js code comment, never surfaced. Severity corrected down: the app is explicitly single-user ("built for exactly one user", app.js:1294) with in-person onboarding, and the fix is one line of copy.

cluster.title: Goal target input silently discards typed values — plan saves the prefilled 158 default
cluster.description: Typing a target and hitting Start plan often reverts the field to 158.0 and commits the default: f05 got 158 instead of 150, f09 158 instead of 128, f10 twice with a 'Plan set' toast; f01/f06 only succeeded on a second attempt; f03's '+' stepper corrupted the value to 672 with the projection line frozen. f08 found the likely root cause: two identical inputs in the DOM, one hidden, and fills landing in the hidden one.
quotes: f09: "it ignored the goal I typed"
f03: "Typed 172 and the field snapped back to 158.0; after one '+' tap the field's value became 672"
f10: "Typed 195, hit Start plan, box reverted to 158.0 while a 'Plan set' toast showed"

votes.evidence: js/app.js:156 (hidden look-alike #w-val), css/app.css:275 (.pi-expand display:none), index.html:109 (#sheet after #app in DOM order), js/app.js:1139-1141 (158.0 default = 165−7), js/app.js:1198-1206 (save reads #g-val, 'Plan set' toast), js/app.js:1191-1196 (stepper = parseFloat+0.5, cannot yield 672), js/app.js:1185-1190 (type toggle repaint discards typed value)
votes.explanation: f08's dual-input diagnosis is real: hidden Today-tab stepper #w-val precedes the sheet's identical #g-val, so selector-based fills land in the hidden input and save commits the untouched 158.0 default. But the goal input never discards values actually typed into it — no code reverts #g-val, and display:none inputs can't receive human focus, so a real user typing on the phone commits correctly. The 672 corruption is impossible per the stepper code. Only the weight/BF toggle genuinely discards typed input.

votes.evidence: js/app.js:1133,1139-1141 (165 fallback − 7 on cut = 158.0 prefill); js/app.js:1165,1198-1206 (no select-on-focus; parseFloat+round saves 158.0, "Plan set" toast); js/app.js:156 vs 1165 + index.html:21,109 + css/app.css:275 (identical hidden input.val #w-val earlier in DOM captures generic fills); js/app.js:1185-1190 (segment toggle repaints, discarding typed value)
votes.explanation: Fresh users get exactly 158.0 prefilled (165 fallback minus 7 on cut). #g-val has no select-on-focus or clear, so natural append-typing yields "158.0150"; save parses, rounds to 158.0, passes validation, and toasts "Plan set" — silently committing the default. f08's root cause is real: the Today weigh card's markup-identical unlabeled input.val (#w-val) sits hidden (display:none) earlier in the DOM, so generic fills land there. Type-toggle repaint also discards typed values. Only f03's "672" is unreproducible.

Severity stays minor: the app declares itself "built for exactly one user" (app.js:1294) — a gym-literate user for whom this is native vocabulary; only the More-tab dev jargon (sync token, API key) is real friction.

cluster.title: Plan ladder and red framing punish steady progress: unlabeled W-chips, shortfall dumped into current week, scolding tone
cluster.description: The W1-W7 chips never say whether they're targets or actuals and render mostly red, making six faithful weeks read as six failures (s03); the current-week box silently absorbs the accumulated shortfall, demanding 2.3-2.6 lb in one week while the caption still says 1.1 lb/wk and the note says 'Same plan' (s01, s03); s07 found the red-heavy 'behind plan / execution slipped' presentation nearly convinced her that genuinely good news (-0.8 lb/wk for 6 weeks) was bad.
quotes: Ruth: "red styling makes six faithful weeks look like six failures"
Nora: "the ladder tells me to crash-cut while the text tells me to change nothing"
Elaine: "The negative framing nearly convinced me the news was bad when it wasn't."

votes.evidence: js/app.js:893-905 (chips show actual-or-expected with only 'W{n}' label; red 'behind plan' stand-line; caption fixed lb/wk), js/plan.js:97-103,108,124-125 (fixed line never re-baselines; hit = actual ≤ expected+0.2 cumulative), js/plan.js:207-214 ('Same plan' notes), css/app.css:326-331 (.mile.miss red)
votes.explanation: Real. Chips mix actuals (completed) and plan-line targets (current/future) with no label. The hit test compares against a fixed cumulative line with 0.2 lb tolerance, so a steady -0.8 vs -1.1 lb/wk cut turns every week red and stuffs the accrued shortfall into the current-week chip (~2.3-2.6 lb implied) while the caption states 1.1 lb/wk and notes say 'Same plan'. Only overstatement: 'execution slipped' fires solely on genuine non-adherence; adherent misses get a calibration message.

cluster.title: Day-boundary/date handling shaky: entries stamped on tomorrow, ambiguous credit for late-night logging
cluster.description: f02 (an 8am session) found a weigh-in dated Jul 16 while the header still said Wednesday Jul 15 — the calendar rolled ahead mid-session. s05, logging just after midnight, couldn't tell which day her steps were credited to (sleep was date-labeled 'Wed night · Jul 15', steps weren't, and Wednesday's eat-healthy question had vanished). The phantom seed entry also carried different dates for different testers (Jul 15 vs Jul 16), suggesting timezone-dependent date math.
quotes: Frank: "stamped an entry on a day that hadn't happened yet for me"
Dana: "my Wednesday step count may have been filed under Thursday"

votes.evidence: js/app.js:59-64, js/app.js:1391-1399, js/app.js:277, js/app.js:320, js/app.js:197, js/app.js:201, js/app.js:210, js/plan.js:253-274, js/store.js:7-12, js/store.js:37, js/sync.js:74, js/app.js:782-831
votes.explanation: Symptoms real; mechanism misdescribed. No midnight-rollover handling: views compute today once per render (app.js:59-64), re-rendering only when sync applies data (app.js:1391-1399), and handlers write the stale closure (app.js:277,320) — so cross-midnight sessions show yesterday's header beside new-day entries, with ambiguous credit. Steps uniquely lacks a date label (app.js:210 vs 197,201); seed weigh-in stamps first-open date (store.js:37). But no timezone-math bug — all local (store.js:7-12), future dates blocked (sync.js:74) — and day-sheet backfill exists (app.js:783).

cluster.title: No lapse recovery for returning users: gap unacknowledged, projection skewed by it, stale first-run copy
cluster.description: After 18 days away, s02 got no welcome-back or rebase offer — just 'NEXT UP: Pull', a red '3.7 lb behind', and a pace projection of 'Jan 12' computed across the no-data flatline with no caveat. The weigh-in card still read 'First entry sets the baseline' despite 27 June weigh-ins. (His return-day impossible weekly target is counted under c19.)
quotes: Sam: "After 2.5 weeks away the dashboard resumes as if nothing happened"
Sam: "The January date comes from my 18-day no-data flatline and lands like a verdict"

votes.evidence: js/app.js:183 (weigh sub falls back to 'First entry sets the baseline' whenever rolling7Avg is null — store.js:153-161 returns null after 7 entry-less days); js/plan.js:97-103,223-227 (expectedAvg marches through the gap while cur falls back to the 18-day-old lastWeight → inflated behindLb, red via app.js:241-243 + css/app.css:253); js/plan.js:232-243 + app.js:905 (14-day trailing window spans the empty gap, 'your pace says {date}' rendered with no caveat, cap 78 wks); grep 'welcome|lapse|gap|rebase' — no lapse-recovery code exists.
votes.explanation: All three behaviors are real. No absence detection exists anywhere; rotation resumes verbatim (store.js:77-81) and the only rebase path is a manual goal edit (plan.js:49). The projection's trailing rate averages across the empty 14-day window uncaveated, and the first-run baseline copy shows for anyone 8+ days lapsed. The lone mitigation — Trends' one-week 'plan is blind without the scale' note (plan.js:200) — is single-week, Trends-only, and offers no rebase, so the testers' account stands.

cluster.title: App navigated itself to the Food tab mid-checklist
cluster.description: Right after f01 answered the Eat-healthy question, the view flipped to the Food tab on its own and she had to tap Today to continue with steps. Possibly an intentional shortcut, but it read as the app changing screens by itself.
quotes: f01: "the app bounced me back to the Food tab on its own; I had to tap Today again"

votes.evidence: js/app.js:275-282 (ateHealthy Yes/No handler: setCheckin/buzz/render/toast only); js/app.js:201 (data-food-go "log the day →" link inside the Eat-healthy row); js/app.js:286 (show('food') fires only on that link); js/app.js:43-51 (only other tab switches: tab bar clicks)
votes.explanation: Spontaneous navigation cannot occur: answering the Eat-healthy question never calls show(); render() stays on Today. The tab flip is real but user-triggered — a tiny "log the day →" link sits directly under the question label in the same row, so f01 almost certainly grazed it. Misdescribed as autonomous, but the co-located link inviting accidental taps mid-checklist is a genuine papercut.

cluster.title: Greeting shows the wrong time of day
cluster.description: 'Evening, Noof' at 8am (f02), 'Morning, Noof' around midnight (f04) and in the afternoon (f10). Small, but it was three users' literal first impression and compounded the wrong-name problem (c01).
quotes: Frank: "it was 8am and my name is Frank"
Derek: "Opened the app around midnight and it said 'Morning, Noof'"
f10: "Screen said 'Morning, Noof' in the afternoon."

votes.evidence: js/app.js:53, js/app.js:61-63, js/app.js:1391-1399
votes.explanation: Both mechanisms are real. app.js:53 maps h<12 to 'morning', so a fresh open just after midnight shows "Morning, Noof" (f04). The greeting is computed only inside renderers.today (app.js:61-63); on resume, visibilitychange re-renders only if Sync.pull returns new weight/steps (app.js:1391-1399), so a PWA backgrounded since evening or morning keeps its stale greeting — explaining "Evening" at 8am (f02) and "Morning" in the afternoon (f10). No timer or focus re-render exists.

reports.missionNotes: Logged the full day: workout as Cardio (one tap, satisfying), sleep Yes, "eat healthy" Yes (real food logging was walled off behind an Anthropic API key so I only got the binary), steps 9,400 via the inline stepper, and weight — which I had to correct from a pre-filled 165.0 lb to my real 152. Set a goal of 137 lb; the app projected 29 weeks because its plan line "started Jul 15 at 165.0 lb," a weight I never gave it, and then showed a "13.0 lb ahead" badge on day one. The ring is the weekly workout counter (1/5, "4 to go this week," "0 lifts + 1 cardio"). Reminders: a "9pm push" exists but only if you paste a "sync token" I don't have, so no, it will not remind me tomorrow. Home-screen decision: delete — the More page literally says "built for exactly one user" and that user is Noof, not me.

confusionPoints.what: Greeted as 'Evening, Noof' with a Push day and a 5/week plan already assigned — no signup or onboarding ever asked my name, weight, or level, so I thought I'd been given someone else's account.

Target pre-filled to 158.0 lb and my 137 target projected '~29 wks · lands Jan 29' — made no sense for a 15 lb cut until I later learned the app assumes a 165 lb starting weight it never asked me for.
Typed 137 into the target field and it still displayed 158.0; had to enter it a second time before it stuck.
'Runs on your own API key, costs about a cent a day' + 'Add API key in More' — I don't know what an API key is; there was no way to just log food without one.
After answering the Eat-healthy question the app bounced me back to the Food tab on its own; I had to tap Today again to continue with steps.
'13.0 lb ahead' on the day I started the plan — flattering but obviously wrong; caused by the invented 165 lb baseline.
The 9pm reminder push requires pasting a 'sync token' into a bare field — no link, no explanation of what it is or where to get one.

issues.title: No onboarding — app assumes a pre-existing identity and body profile
issues.what: First open drops me on a dashboard greeting me as 'Noof' with a Push/Pull/Legs rotation, a 5-session weekly goal, 165.0 lb weight, and 18% body fat already set. About section says 'built for exactly one user.' Nothing ever asked who I am.
issues.expected: A first-run flow asking my name, current weight, experience level, and weekly capacity before prescribing anything.

issues.title: Goal plan anchored to invented 165 lb baseline, ignores my actual logged weight
issues.what: Setting a 137 lb target projected ~29 weeks and, after I logged my real weight (152) the same day, showed '13.0 lb ahead.' More tab confirms 'Line started Jul 15 at 165.0 lb' — a number I never entered.
issues.expected: The plan should start from the weight I actually log (152), giving a ~15-week projection and no fake 'ahead' credit.

issues.title: Food logging unusable without a personal Anthropic API key
issues.what: The entire Food feature is a pitch card saying it runs on my own API key from console.anthropic.com, with 'Add API key in More' as the only action. As a normal phone user I cannot log what I ate beyond the Yes/No question.
issues.expected: Some built-in way to log food (text list, even manual calories) without creating a developer account and paying for API access.

issues.title: Daily reminders locked behind an unexplained 'sync token'
issues.what: The 9pm reminder push, auto-steps, and auto weigh-ins all require pasting a 'sync token' into a field with no link or explanation. I have no token, so the app cannot remind me tomorrow.
issues.expected: Notifications I can switch on with a toggle and a permission prompt, or at minimum a 'what is this / get one here' link.

issues.title: Today's entry pre-populated with false weight and body fat presented as logged data
issues.screen: Today (All caught up chip) / weight sheet
issues.what: Without me entering anything, the day summary displayed 'Weight 165.0 lb' and the edit sheet showed body fat 18% — fabricated values shown as if they were my data.
issues.expected: Weight and body fat blank until I enter them, with a prompt to log my first weigh-in.

issues.title: Goal target input reverts first typed value
issues.what: Typed 137 over the pre-filled 158.0 and the field still showed 158.0; only a second attempt registered (there appear to be two identical inputs, one hidden).
issues.expected: The value I type sticks the first time.

issues.title: App navigated itself back to the Food tab mid-logging
issues.what: While answering the checklist (right around the Eat-healthy Yes / steps tap), the view flipped to the Food tab on its own and I had to tap Today to get back.
issues.expected: Stay on Today while I work through the checklist.

issues.title: Scary data-loss warning in More
issues.what: '⚠ Never backed up. iOS can evict web-app data — export a backup' — tells me my logs can just vanish unless I manually export a file.
issues.expected: Apps should keep my data without homework; if backup matters this much, offer automatic backup or a gentler one-tap flow.

topImprovements: Add a real first-run onboarding (name, current weight, level, sessions/week) and anchor the goal line to my first logged weight instead of a hardcoded 165 lb profile.
Make food logging and the 9pm reminder work out of the box — no API keys or sync tokens — or hide those sections entirely until they're configured instead of showing dead-end CTAs.
reports.verbatim: Okay the vibe is immaculate — dark mode, lime ring, and logging my whole day was like five taps, which is more than I can say for most fitness apps. But it kept calling me Noof, told me I weigh 165 with 18% body fat (I never said ANY of that), and the food AI, the coach, and the reminders all want some developer API key or 'sync token' I will never have. Then the About page said 'built for exactly one user' — bestie, that user is not me, so it's cute but it's getting deleted.

reports.missionNotes: Logged sleep (one tap, worked instantly), logged my weight (205 lb — but only after hunting through Fuel and More before finding that you tap a day under Trends "Recent entries"), logged my morning walk as a Cardio session, and eventually set a 190 lb goal — though the app first refused it because it believed I weighed 165, a number I never gave it. Found the weight-over-time chart under Trends (BODY WEIGHT — 7-DAY AVG). Food logging failed: the Food tab demands a "Claude API key" from console.anthropic.com and offers no plain way to write down what I ate; my fallback Yes on "Eat healthy today?" never checked off, and my step count (4,200) never stuck either despite repeated tries — those two questions were still sitting there unanswered when I finished. A weigh-in dated Jul 16 (tomorrow) also appeared in my entries that I never knowingly made.

confusionPoints.what: Greeting said "Evening, Noof" — it was 8am and my name is Frank. I spent the whole session wondering whose app I was using (the About section later said "built for exactly one user").
Big word "Push" with buttons Pull / Legs / Cardio / Recovery and a green Done button. No explanation of what Push means or what Done would record — I hadn't done anything yet.

It opened showing 158.0 lb and a line about "my prescription" and a landing date, before ever asking what I currently weigh. When I typed 190 it said my target must be below "your 165.0 lb average" — an average of numbers I never entered.
Pressing Start plan with an invalid target did nothing at all — no message, no shake, nothing. I couldn't tell if I'd pressed it.

The only thing on the page is a paragraph about Claude, an API key, and "costs about a cent a day," plus one button sending me to More. I don't know what an API key is, and there was no simple place to write down my oatmeal.
A box asking for a "sync token." No idea what that is or where one comes from; second secret code the app wanted from me.
An entire meal schedule that isn't mine — Office breakfast, Catered lunch, 7pm lift, whey shake — plus jargon like "165g P" and "60 / 59g fat." I'm a retired man at a kitchen table.
It asks at breakfast time whether I ate healthy TODAY. The day isn't over — an accountant doesn't close the books at 8:30 in the morning.
Nothing on Today, Fuel, or More says "log weight." I only found it because Trends has "RECENT ENTRIES — TAP A DAY TO EDIT." Editing an existing stranger's entry was the only door in.
"⚠ Never backed up. iOS can evict web-app data" — evicted, like a tenant? Alarming words on my first day, with no plain explanation of what to do about it.
After logging one session against my goal of 3 per week, it said "1 to go this week." Three minus one is two. The arithmetic on screen didn't add up for me.

issues.title: App arrives pre-filled with another person's identity and data
issues.what: First open greeted me as "Noof," today's date already had a weigh-in of 165.0 lb · 18% I never made, the Fuel tab held a full meal plan referencing catered office lunches and a 7pm lift, and About says "built for exactly one user." I had to overwrite the stranger's weigh-in to make the app mine.
issues.expected: A brand-new app should ask my name and current weight and start with an empty ledger.

issues.title: Goal setup blocks my target using a weight I never provided, and Start plan fails silently
issues.what: Default target was 158 lb; entering 190 produced "Cutting — target must be below your 165.0 lb average," and pressing Start plan did nothing — no error message, no feedback. Only after I found and edited the weigh-in to my real 205 lb did the same 190 goal get accepted.
issues.expected: Ask my current weight first, or at least explain what to do and give clear feedback when the button won't work.

issues.title: Cannot log food without obtaining a Claude API key from a developer website
issues.what: The Food tab's only content is an explanation that logging runs on "your own API key" costing "about a cent a day," with a single button to More, where I'm told to get a key at console.anthropic.com. I have no key and no idea how to get one, so I could not write down what I ate at all from that screen.
issues.expected: A plain fallback — type what I ate as a note, or at least the Yes/No — without any keys, websites, or payments.

issues.title: Eat healthy and steps answers never register on the Today checklist
issues.what: I tapped Yes on "Eat healthy today?" and entered my step count through "enter count" (tried twice, including typing 4200 and pressing Save); both questions remained unanswered on the list. Only the Sleep question checked itself off properly.
issues.expected: Every question should check off on one tap, the way the sleep one did.

issues.title: Pop-up windows linger and stack instead of closing cleanly
issues.screen: Goal sheet / weight editor / steps entry
issues.what: The goal window seemed not to close when I tapped outside it (I couldn't tell for a while), the weight editor appeared to stay around after Save, and at one point three separate number boxes (weight 205, steps 8000, goal 190) were live at once, so my typing landed in the wrong boxes.
issues.expected: One window at a time; tapping outside or Save should visibly close it.

issues.title: A weigh-in dated tomorrow (Jul 16) appeared in my entries
issues.what: While the header still said Wednesday, July 15, my recent entries showed both "Jul 15 205.0 lb" and a "Jul 16 205.0 lb" I never deliberately created — the app's calendar rolled ahead mid-session and stamped an entry on a day that hadn't happened yet for me.
issues.expected: Entries only on the day I made them, matching the date shown at the top.

issues.title: Greeting shows wrong time of day
issues.what: At my kitchen table in the morning the app said "Evening, Noof."
issues.expected: "Morning" in the morning, and my own name once the app knows it.

issues.title: Gym and tech jargon throughout with no beginner explanations
issues.what: Push/Pull/Legs, "165g P," "biggest non-gym lever," "plan engine is idle," "LFG," "sync token," "iOS can evict web-app data" — none of it is explained anywhere I could find.
issues.expected: Plain words, or a tap-to-explain for terms a beginner won't know.

issues.title: Stranger's 18% body fat stayed attached to my weigh-in
issues.what: The pre-filled 18% body fat remained on my entry after I changed the weight to 205; I don't know my body fat and saw no way to mark it as unknown.
issues.expected: Let me leave body fat blank instead of inheriting someone else's number.

topImprovements: Add a first-run setup that asks name and current weight, starts the ledger empty (no previous owner's greeting, weigh-in, or meal plan), and puts an obvious "Log weight" button on the Today screen instead of hiding entry behind tapping a row under Trends.

Make food logging work with zero keys or accounts — a simple text note plus the Yes/No — and fix the Today checklist so Eat healthy and steps answers register and check off as reliably as the Sleep question.
reports.verbatim: "It feels like I moved into somebody else's house — his name's on the mailbox, his lunches are in the fridge, and the scale remembered his weight. Once I found where to write my own weight down, that chart is exactly what my doctor wants to see, so it stays on the home screen for now — but my son's going to have to sort out this API key nonsense and the questions that won't stay answered."

reports.missionNotes: Logged everything the app allows: Push workout (one tap, with undo toast), sleep (Yes), eat-healthy (Yes — real food logging is gated behind an Anthropic API key I don't have), 8,400 steps via "enter count", and my weight 131.0 lb by editing a pre-seeded "165.0 lb · 18%" entry under Trends > Recent Entries. Privacy answers: data lives on-device in browser storage ("stored only on this phone", "encrypted on this phone", "iOS can evict web-app data — export a backup"); nothing leaves by default; two opt-in cloud paths (Claude API with your own key; a token-gated "private worker" for reminders/steps/scale sync) are inert without credentials. Data out: an Export button exists (log + PIN-encrypted photos) but gave no visible confirmation when pressed. Data deletion: no in-app delete/reset control anywhere I found. Verdict: it does not stay on my home screen — it's transparently someone else's single-user app.

confusionPoints.what: No cancel/close affordance: Escape, backdrop taps, and clicking outside all failed to dismiss it; the only exits were 'Start plan' or reloading the whole app, which is what I did.

confusionPoints.where: Set your goal sheet, target weight field
confusionPoints.what: Typed 172 and the field snapped back to 158.0; after one '+' tap the field's value became 672, while the prescription line stayed frozen at '−1.0 lb/wk · ~8 wks · lands Sep 3' regardless of the number.

Wanted to log what I ate; the entire tab is a pitch for the Claude API key feature with an 'Add API key in More' button — no manual entry path, so I couldn't tell how to log food without a key (answer: you can't, only the Yes/No on Today).
Not on Today, not on Fuel, not on More; finally found it buried in Trends as 'Recent entries — tap a day to edit', editing a weigh-in entry I never created.
Pressed Export and saw no confirmation, toast, or change to the 'Never backed up' warning that I could verify — unclear whether a file was actually produced.

'Your private worker' wants a 'sync token' — no explanation of what server this is, who runs it, or what data it would see; I don't have a token so I left it alone, but the wording is opaque for something that auto-collects steps and weigh-ins.
Greeted as 'Morning, Noof' with zero onboarding — no name, units, weight, or goal questions — so for a minute I wasn't sure if I'd been handed someone's logged-in account.

issues.title: Fresh install is pre-loaded with another person's identity and data
issues.what: After a full reset the app greeted me as 'Noof', Trends contained a weigh-in for today (165.0 lb · 18%) that I never entered, and Fuel showed a complete cut meal plan built 'from your 165 lb average' (Chipotle bowl, beef jerky, 2,090 kcal targets). About section confirms 'built for exactly one user'.
issues.expected: A first-run setup asking my name, units, current weight, and goal — and an empty log. My history should never contain fabricated entries; a seeded weigh-in poisons the 7-day average and every downstream target.

issues.title: Goal sheet cannot be dismissed
issues.what: The sheet has no close/cancel button; pressing Escape and tapping the backdrop (verified with a real click on the backdrop element) do nothing. The only on-screen exit is 'Start plan'. I had to reload the app to get out.
issues.expected: Tap outside, swipe down, or an explicit Cancel to dismiss without committing to a plan.

issues.title: Goal weight input ignores typed values and corrupts via stepper
issues.what: Typed 172 into the target-weight box; it reverted to 158.0. One tap of '+' then left the field holding 672. The prescription line ('−1.0 lb/wk · ~8 wks · lands Sep 3') never recalculated in response to any of this.
issues.expected: Typed input should stick, steppers should increment by a sane amount from the visible value, and the projection line should recompute from the actual field value.

issues.title: Food logging is impossible without an Anthropic API key
issues.what: The Food tab offers only the Claude-powered dictation flow ('Runs on your own API key') and an 'Add API key in More' button. With no key there is no manual food entry at all — the only fallback is the binary 'Eat healthy today? Yes/No' on Today.
issues.expected: A basic manual entry path (even just a text note or simple list) that works without any cloud service or key.

issues.title: No in-app way to delete data or reset the app
issues.what: More offers Export and Import under Backup, but no 'delete my data', 'clear history', or factory-reset control anywhere. For an app holding weight, body fat, and encrypted progress photos, the only wipe path is browser-level site-data clearing, which the app never mentions.
issues.expected: An explicit delete/reset control next to Export, with a confirmation step.

issues.title: Export gives no visible confirmation
issues.what: Pressed Export; no toast, dialog, or status change appeared that I could find, and the 'Never backed up' warning state was not observably updated.
issues.expected: A visible 'Backup exported' confirmation or an updated 'last backed up' timestamp.

issues.title: Defaults assume cutting, not muscle gain
issues.what: Goal sheet offers only target weight/body fat with a '−1.0 lb/wk — my prescription' framing; Trends header reads 'Cutting — down and to the right' on a fresh install; Fuel targets are labeled 'Cut targets'. A Cut/Bulk toggle exists on Trends but everything defaults to cutting for a brand-new user whose goal was never asked.
issues.expected: Ask goal direction during setup, or at least neutral defaults until a goal is chosen.

issues.title: Sync feature is opaque about where data goes
issues.what: Copy says 'Your private worker: a 9pm push... steps that log themselves, and scale weigh-ins that fill in on their own' plus a bare 'sync token' field. No mention of what endpoint this is, who operates it, or what data it can read/write. (It is off by default without a token, which is good.)
issues.expected: One sentence naming the service, what data flows to it, and that it's optional — the API-key section's 'Stored only on this phone' is the model to copy.

topImprovements: Add a real first-run: ask name, units, current weight, and goal direction (gain/cut), start with an empty log — never pre-seed weigh-ins or meal plans the user didn't enter — and put a 'delete all my data' control next to Export.

Make the goal sheet dismissible (backdrop tap/Cancel) and fix its input handling (typed values revert, stepper produced 672, projection line never updates), and give Food a manual no-API-key entry path.
reports.verbatim: Credit where due: this is more private than 99 percent of fitness apps — everything on the phone, bring your own API key, export to one file, and the cloud bits stay off until you opt in. But it greeted me as Noof, my 'fresh' log already contained his 165 lb weigh-in and his Chipotle meal plan, the goal sheet trapped me until I force-reloaded, and there's no delete button anywhere. It's a lovely, fast little app that is very openly not mine — so no, it doesn't stay on my home screen.

reports.missionNotes: Logged the full day: sleep (one-tap Yes), steps (used "enter count" to log real 4,600 — app marked it "under target"), food (the AI food log was locked behind a bring-your-own Claude API key, so fell back to the binary "Eat healthy? No"), and weight (231 lb — only found the entry field by accident inside History → tap day → bottom sheet, pre-filled with the owner's 165). Did not log a workout because Derek genuinely didn't train, and the app handled that fine (no phantom entries). Home-screen verdict: stays for now purely because logging a day takes under a minute; week-two verdict: no — the app is openly hardcoded for one other user (name, meal plan, targets) and its day-one "5 to go, 4 days left" scoreboard already declares the week unwinnable.

confusionPoints.what: Greeted as "Morning, Noof" with zero onboarding — spent a beat wondering if I'd installed someone else's account or a demo.
Full pre-built meal plan with "Cut targets from your 165 lb average" — I never entered a weight and I weigh 231, so whose plan is this and can I change it? No visible way to.
"Log the day" dead-ends into "Add API key in More" — unclear why a food diary needs me to go get a developer key from console.anthropic.com, and no non-AI fallback offered on that screen.
A bare "sync token" field with no hint where a token comes from — stared at it, gave up.
Checked Today, Fuel, and More before stumbling into it via History → tap a day → bottom sheet. Nothing anywhere says weight lives there.
First tap of "Done" appeared to do nothing — no toast, sheet stayed open — so I couldn't tell whether my 231 lb saved; tapped again and it closed.
"5 to go, 4 days left — today counts" on install day: 5 sessions in 4 days is impossible, so the week reads as already failed before I've done anything.

issues.title: App is hardcoded to another person (Noof) with no onboarding or personalization
issues.screen: Today header, Fuel tab, History day sheet, More (About)
issues.what: Fresh install greeted me as "Morning, Noof", served a complete meal plan built on "your 165 lb average" with the owner's office schedule (buy-lunch day, catered-lunch day), pre-filled the weight field with 165 lb and body fat 18%, and the About section says "built for exactly one user." I was never asked my name, weight, split, or goal.
issues.expected: A first-run setup asking who I am and my numbers, with targets and the meal plan derived from MY weight/goal — or at least editable identity/targets somewhere.

issues.title: Food logging requires bring-your-own Claude API key; Food tab is otherwise a dead end
issues.what: The advertised flow ("Say what you ate once a day — I'll do the math") is unusable until I get an API key from console.anthropic.com and paste it in More, with a note that it costs money. The Food tab offers nothing else — one button pointing to More. I bailed and used the binary Yes/No on Today instead.
issues.expected: Some way to log food out of the box (even plain text saved for later), with the AI/key setup as an optional upgrade explained in-app — not a hard wall at 11pm.

issues.title: Manual weight entry is buried and pre-filled with the owner's stats
issues.screen: History → tap a day → day sheet
issues.what: Weight/body-fat entry exists only inside the History calendar's per-day sheet; Today, Fuel, and More have no weight entry (More only mentions auto weigh-ins via a sync token I don't have). The field came pre-showing 165 lb / 18% — not my numbers — so a careless Done could record someone else's weight.
issues.expected: A visible "log weight" affordance on the Today screen (alongside sleep/steps/food), defaulting to blank or my last entry.

issues.title: Day-one weekly target is mathematically impossible and reads as already failed
issues.what: On install day (Thursday) the plan says "5 to go, 4 days left — today counts" with a 0/5 ring — 5 sessions cannot fit in 4 days, so the app's first message to a lapsed lifter is that the week is unwinnable.
issues.expected: Prorate the first partial week (e.g., "3 possible this week — start with 1") or start counting from install day.

issues.title: Day sheet Done button gives no feedback on first tap
issues.what: After editing weight, tapping Done produced no toast and the sheet stayed open; only a second tap closed it. I had no confirmation my weight was saved (it turned out it was).
issues.expected: One tap closes the sheet or shows a "saved" confirmation like the other logging actions do.

issues.title: Greeting says "Morning" during a midnight session
issues.what: Opened the app around midnight and it said "Morning, Noof" — the greeting doesn't match when I'm actually using it (and the name isn't mine either).
issues.expected: Time-appropriate greeting ("Late night?" would honestly land better for this audience).

topImprovements: Add a first-run onboarding (name, weight, goal, weekly split) and derive greeting, Fuel targets/meal plan, and day-sheet defaults from it — right now everything is another person's data and the app says so ("built for exactly one user").

Make the daily core loop fully self-serve with zero keys: put weight entry (and the steps-style count option) on the Today screen, give Food a no-API-key fallback, and prorate the first week's target so day one doesn't start as a guaranteed failure.
reports.verbatim: "The one-tap yes/no logging is legit — I logged sleep, steps, food, and weight in about a minute flat, which is more than any app has gotten out of me in two years. But it kept calling me Noof, fed me a 165-pound dude's meal plan, wanted an API key to log a chicken bowl, and told me on day one I'd already blown the week. It'd survive to Sunday, not to week two."

reports.missionNotes: Logged everything: sleep (No), steps (6,200 via 'enter count'), eat-healthy (No — actual food logging is locked behind an Anthropic API key, so the binary answer was the only option), workout (Recovery + Done — the app confirmed it but it didn't count toward the 5/week target), and weight (171.8 lb — only findable by tapping a pre-existing phantom entry of '165.0 lb · 18%' under Trends > Recent Entries). Set a goal, but the app saved its default 158 instead of the 150 I typed. Verdict on the guilt question: individual logging is gentle (every answer gets a calm 'noted · tap to undo', no lectures), but the plan framing is brutal — '5 to go, 4 days left — today counts' from the first second, and after I entered my real weight it declared me '6.8 lb behind plan' on day one because the plan was built from a fabricated 165 lb baseline. After a missed week this app would bury me in behind-ness. Would not stay on my home screen: it is openly someone else's app ('Morning, Noof', their meal plan, About says 'built for exactly one user').

confusionPoints.what: App greeted me 'Morning, Noof' with a full plan already running (NEXT UP: Push, 5 to go, 4 days left). No onboarding, never asked my name/weight/level — I thought I'd been given someone else's account.
I typed 150 as my target but the box visibly reverted to 158.0 after saving, and the toast just said 'Plan set' — I had no idea which number it kept (More tab later confirmed: it kept 158, not mine).
No X/close button; tapping the dark backdrop and tapping tab-bar items did not dismiss it. It stayed open the entire session (it also silently ate my first tap on 'log the day').
Food logging requires 'your own API key' from console.anthropic.com. I don't have one and don't know what it is; there is no manual type-what-you-ate fallback on that screen.
No weight entry on Today, Fuel, or More (More only mentions auto scale weigh-ins). I only found it by accident: Trends > 'Recent entries — tap a day to edit' on an entry I never created.
It's a fully pre-built stranger's meal plan ('Cut targets from your 165 lb average', 7am/7pm lifts, Chipotle order, beef jerky top-up) — nothing indicated how to make it about me.

confusionPoints.where: Today plan line after logging Recovery
confusionPoints.what: Counter stayed '0/5 — 5 to go, 4 days left' (impossible math) and showed '0 lifts · 1 recovery' — so my recovery day counts for nothing toward the week.

issues.title: App ships pre-loaded with another person's identity and data; no onboarding for a new user
issues.what: First open greets 'Morning, Noof', a 5-lifts/week plan is already active, Fuel contains a complete stranger's meal plan built on a '165 lb average', and About says 'built for exactly one user. LFG.' I was never asked my name, weight, or capacity.
issues.expected: A first-run setup asking who I am, my current weight, and how many sessions/week I can realistically do — a blank slate that becomes MY app.

issues.title: Goal sheet discards the typed target weight and saves its default
issues.screen: Set your goal sheet (verified in More > GOAL)
issues.what: I typed 150 into the target box and hit Start plan; the box reverted to 158.0 and More later showed 'Cut to 158.0 lb'. My input was silently thrown away.
issues.expected: The number I typed becomes the goal, or at minimum the confirmation states the saved value.

issues.title: 'Set your goal' sheet cannot be dismissed and intercepts taps
issues.screen: Set your goal sheet (persists over all tabs)
issues.what: No close control; tapping the backdrop and tapping the tab bar did not close it. It remained open across Today/Food/Fuel/More/Trends for the whole session, and my first real tap on 'log the day' did nothing (swallowed) until I retried.
issues.expected: Tap outside or an X closes the sheet, and it certainly closes after 'Plan set'.

issues.title: Food logging requires a personal Anthropic API key with no manual fallback
issues.what: The entire tab is one paragraph telling me to get an API key at console.anthropic.com ('costs about a cent a day') and 'Add API key in More'. I couldn't log what I ate at all; only the Yes/No 'eat healthy' toggle on Today was available.
issues.expected: A way to record food without developer credentials — even a plain text note — with the AI analysis as an optional upgrade.

issues.title: Plan math built on fabricated baseline declares me '6.8 lb behind plan' on day one
issues.what: A weigh-in of '165.0 lb · 18%' existed for today that I never entered; the cut plan used it as the start line. When I corrected today's weight to my real 171.8, the header immediately showed '13.8 lb to go · 6.8 lb behind plan' — on my first day, after logging everything honestly.
issues.expected: Day one IS the baseline. Entering my first real weight should start the line there, not judge me against a number the app invented.

issues.title: Week framing is pre-failed: '5 to go, 4 days left — today counts', and Recovery doesn't count
issues.what: From first open the card demanded 5 sessions with 4 days left (impossible), and after logging a Recovery it still read 0/5 with '0 lifts · 1 recovery'.
issues.expected: A weekly target that starts from when I joined, and gentler credit — recovery/cardio visibly moving the needle instead of a separate zero.

issues.title: Phantom weigh-in pre-seeded for today
issues.what: 'Jul 16 — 165.0 lb · 18%' existed before I ever logged weight.
issues.expected: No data in my log that I didn't create (or a device import clearly labeled as such).

issues.title: No discoverable place to log weight
issues.what: Weight isn't loggable from Today or More (More only advertises auto scale sync); I found entry only via editing an existing day in Trends.
issues.expected: A 'log weight' affordance on the Today screen next to sleep/steps/eat.

topImprovements: Add a real first-run onboarding that asks my name, current weight, and realistic sessions/week — and honor the numbers I type (the goal sheet discarded my 150 for its 158 default; the plan baseline was a weight I never gave). Until then every screen, from 'Morning, Noof' to '6.8 lb behind plan', belongs to someone else.

Make the daily loop fully one-thumb self-sufficient: a no-API-key food fallback (even a one-line text note) and a visible 'log weight' button on Today, plus a close X on bottom sheets so they can't sit there eating taps.
reports.verbatim: The little 'noted, tap to undo' answers were sweet and I could do them with my thumb while she slept — but the app kept telling me I was behind a plan I never agreed to, on a body it made up, in an app that literally greets me by someone else's name. Food logging wants me to buy an API key from a developer console — I have a five-month-old. It says it right there in About: built for exactly one user, and she isn't me.

reports.missionNotes: Set the 10 lb goal and got a genuinely good plan (195 -> 185, -1.2 lb/wk, weekly milestones, lands Sep 14), but only after a fight: the app shipped with a fabricated weigh-in (165.0 lb / 18% BF) under someone else's name ("Morning, Noof"), rejected my real 185 target against that invented average, and after I corrected my weight to 195 it declared me "30.0 lb behind plan" on day one until I re-saved the goal. Logged sleep (No), food (No, honest airport eating), 4,500 steps, and my 195 lb weigh-in by editing the phantom entry in Trends. Skipped the workout deliberately — travel day, and I won't tap "Done" on a session I didn't do. Verdict: it stays on the home screen for a trial week — the no-signup privacy and the numbers-first plan won me over despite the messy first ten minutes.

confusionPoints.what: Greeted with 'Morning, Noof' — I'm Tom. No onboarding, no name prompt, no way I could find to make the app mine.

It prescribed a plan against a '165.0 lb average' I never provided, and rejected my real 185 target with 'target must be below your 165.0 lb average' — with no field anywhere in the flow to state my current weight.
Typed 185 into the target box and it snapped back to 158.0; only stuck on the second attempt.
No X, no Cancel, tapping outside did nothing — the only way out I could find was committing a plan I knew was based on a wrong weight.
A weigh-in for today (165.0 lb / 18%) already existed on a freshly installed app; took a moment to realize tapping that phantom entry was the only path to logging my real weight.

My first attempt to update the target to 185 appeared to complete but the plan card still showed the old 155 plan with no error — the save button in edit mode is labeled differently ('Save — restart the line from today') and my first tap did nothing.

issues.title: Fresh install ships with someone else's identity and a fabricated weigh-in
issues.what: Brand-new app greeted me as 'Noof' and Trends already contained a weigh-in for today: 165.0 lb / 18% body fat. I never entered either. All goal math validated against this invented weight.
issues.expected: First run asks my name and current weight (or prompts a first weigh-in) and starts with zero data.

issues.title: Goal flow never asks current weight and rejects real targets
issues.what: Wanted 195 -> 185. The sheet refused 185 ('Cutting — target must be below your 165.0 lb average') and offered no way to correct the current weight. I had to abandon the flow, find the weigh-in editor on a different tab, then redo the goal from scratch.
issues.expected: Goal setup asks or confirms current weight inline, then validates the target against what I actually weigh.

issues.title: Plan doesn't rebase after a corrected weigh-in — '30.0 lb behind plan' on day one
issues.what: After I fixed my weight to 195, the plan kept its old 165-based milestones and showed '40.0 lb to go · 30.0 lb behind plan' before I had done a single thing. Only manually re-saving the goal rebuilt the milestones.
issues.expected: A one-tap offer to restart the plan line from the new weight, not a day-one guilt trip based on stale numbers.

issues.title: Goal sheet has no cancel or close
issues.what: No X, no Cancel button, tapping outside the sheet did not dismiss it. I was cornered into starting a plan I knew was wrong (target 155) just to get out of the sheet.
issues.expected: A close/X or tap-outside dismissal on every sheet; never force a commit to exit.

issues.title: Updating a goal silently fails if you miss the renamed save button
issues.what: On my first edit attempt the sheet closed with the plan unchanged (still 155, still 'behind plan') and no error or toast. Second pass I noticed the button is 'Save — restart the line from today' in edit mode and it worked.
issues.expected: One consistent, obvious primary action, and an error or confirmation so a failed save is never silent.

issues.title: Target weight input reverts typed value on first entry
issues.what: Typed 185 over the default 158.0 and the field snapped back to 158.0; the value only held on a second attempt.
issues.expected: Typed value sticks the first time.

issues.title: Content clipped at the right edge of the screen
issues.what: Workout type buttons (Cardio cut to 'C...'), milestone chips, and the weekly calorie value ('2,09...') were visibly cut off at the right edge in both screens I captured.
issues.expected: All values and buttons fully visible or clearly scrollable at my screen width.

topImprovements: Add a 60-second first run: ask my name and current weight, start with zero seeded data, and validate goal targets against what I enter — never against an invented average.
When a new weigh-in moves my baseline, offer 'Restart plan from 195?' in one tap instead of showing '30 lb behind plan' on day one — and give every sheet a cancel/close.
reports.verbatim: The plan card is the best I've seen — a rate, weekly milestones, a landing date, and it never once asked for my email, which I appreciate more than any feature. But it greeted me by someone else's name, invented a scale reading for me, and I spent half my lounge time fighting the goal screen to make the plan about my actual body. Fix the first ten minutes and this thing stays on my home screen for good.

reports.missionNotes: Set a goal (15% BF via the goal sheet), logged a Push workout (one tap), sleep (Yes/No toggle — answered Yes), food (only the binary "Eat healthy today?" — the real Food log is locked behind a bring-your-own Anthropic API key I don't have), steps (9,450 via inline stepper), and weight (129.8 lb / 19% — found only by tapping a day under Trends > Recent entries). Evaluated its tracking against Oura/Whoop: it captures none of what my devices do (no duration, stages, HRV, readiness, strain), sleep is a self-reported yes/no counted as "nights slept well per week," and the goal engine is anchored to a hardcoded 165 lb stranger's baseline that broke the moment I entered my real weight. Decision: it does not stay on my home screen.

confusionPoints.what: Greeted 'Morning, Noof' with zero onboarding — wondered if I'd been handed someone else's account; no name, weight, or device setup ever asked.
Target pre-filled with a prescription ('−1.0 lb/wk — my prescription', lands date, ≈159.2 lb) computed from a current weight (~165 lb / 18%) I never entered; found no way to set MY baseline.

confusionPoints.where: Set your goal sheet after Start plan
confusionPoints.what: Unclear whether the sheet closed/saved — no obvious dismissal; I only trusted it via the toast and the 'on plan' badge, and the sheet content kept lingering in the page structure afterwards.
Whole tab is a single 'Add API key in More' card — I stalled looking for any manual way to log meals; there is none besides the Yes/No toggle on Today.
A 'sync token' field promises auto steps and scale weigh-ins, but there's no hint where a token comes from — dead end.
Checked Today, Fuel, and More before discovering weight entry hides under Trends > 'Recent entries — tap a day to edit'.
A weigh-in 'Jul 16 — 165.0 lb · 18%' already existed on a fresh install; I never logged it and briefly doubted my reset.

confusionPoints.where: Trends plan card after entering real weight
confusionPoints.what: '0.0 lb to go · 35.2 lb ahead of plan' with milestones still counting down from 165 — couldn't tell if my goal was complete, broken, or both.

issues.title: App is hardcoded to another person with no onboarding or profile
issues.what: Fresh install greets me as 'Noof', prescribes 2,090 kcal / 165g protein 'from your 165 lb average', shows a personal meal plan ('Thu — Buy-lunch day', a specific Chipotle order, 'TOMORROW — CATERED-LUNCH DAY'), and About admits 'built for exactly one user'. No screen ever asks who I am or what I weigh.
issues.expected: An onboarding step asking my name, current weight/BF, and schedule — or at minimum editable baseline stats — before prescribing calories and cut targets.

issues.title: Phantom weigh-in seeded on fresh install
issues.what: Before I logged anything, Recent entries showed 'Jul 16 — 165.0 lb · 18%' and the body-weight chart plotted it as real data.
issues.expected: An empty weight history until I log my first weigh-in.

issues.title: Goal engine doesn't recalibrate when real weight contradicts its baseline
issues.what: After I saved my actual 129.8 lb, the cut plan read '129.8 → 159.2 lb · 0.0 lb to go · 35.2 lb ahead of plan' while milestones still ticked down W1 164.6 … W6 159.6 — a 6-week cut marked effectively complete on day one, with a goal weight ABOVE my body weight.
issues.expected: The plan to re-anchor to my entered weight (or prompt me to reset the goal) instead of congratulating me on finishing a cut I never started.

issues.title: Sleep tracking is a single self-reported Yes/No with no analysis
issues.what: Sleep = 'Sleep well last night?' Yes/No, aggregated in Trends as 'Slept well 1 / 5+ nights'. No hours, no stages, no HRV, no readiness, and the SIGNALS section only counts lifts — no correlation between sleep answers and anything.
issues.expected: Coming from Oura/Whoop: at least sleep duration and a trend, ideally a sleep-vs-training correlation. As shipped it's a habit checkbox, not sleep tracking.

issues.title: Food logging and Coach are dead ends without a bring-your-own Anthropic API key
issues.what: Both tabs are a single pitch card ('Runs on your own API key… Add API key in More'). Without a key there is no meal logging at all — the only fallback is the binary 'Eat healthy today?' on Today.
issues.expected: A manual food-entry fallback (even a text note), or a built-in service; a consumer app asking me to provision a developer API key is a wall.

issues.title: Weight entry is buried three levels deep with no signpost
issues.what: The only way to log weight/BF is Trends > 'RECENT ENTRIES — TAP A DAY TO EDIT' > tap a day. Nothing on Today, Fuel, or More points there; I crossed five screens to find it.
issues.expected: A 'log weight' affordance on Today (where every other daily metric lives) or in More.

issues.title: Sync token field gives no way to obtain a token
issues.what: The card promises auto-logged steps and scale weigh-ins via 'your private worker' plus a bare 'sync token' input — no link, no explanation of where a token comes from (the API-key card next to it at least says console.anthropic.com).
issues.expected: A link or one-line instruction for getting the token, or hiding the card for users who can't have one.

issues.title: Workout log carries zero training data
issues.what: A workout is one tap of a type chip (Push/Pull/Legs/Cardio/Recovery). No sets, reps, load, duration, or PRs anywhere — History/Trends can only ever count sessions.
issues.expected: For a performance user, at least optional session details; as-is it's a streak counter for the gym.

issues.title: Goal sheet lingers after Start plan
issues.what: After tapping Start plan the sheet gave no clear dismissal; its content remained present in the page structure across tab switches (visually hidden), and confirmation came only from a toast.
issues.expected: The sheet to visibly close/confirm on save.

topImprovements: Add a real first-run: ask name + current weight/BF and anchor the goal line, macro targets, and weight chart to that — never seed a phantom 165 lb weigh-in, and re-anchor the plan when a logged weight contradicts the baseline.

Make the binary data mean something without a key: sleep hours alongside the Yes/No with a sleep-vs-lifts correlation in SIGNALS, and a manual food-entry fallback so the Food tab isn't empty without an API key.
reports.verbatim: It's genuinely charming — one-tap logging, undo on everything, the honest 'built for exactly one user' copy — but that's the problem: it's Noof's app and I'm just visiting. It greeted me by his name, fed me macros off his 165 lb body, invented a weigh-in I never took, and told me my cut was done the second I entered my real weight. My Oura logs more about my sleep before I'm awake than this app can ever know, so no — it doesn't survive on my home screen.

reports.missionNotes: Logged everything the app allows: Push workout (single tap "Done"), sleep = Yes, ate healthy = Yes, steps = 9,450, weight = 198.6 lb and body fat 15% via the History day editor. The detailed-bench part of the mission hit a hard wall: there is no place anywhere in the app to enter exercises, sets, reps, weights, or RPE — a workout is a one-tap type (Push/Pull/Legs/Cardio/Recovery) and nothing more. Food-in-detail and Coach are both locked behind a bring-your-own API key, which I don't have. Home-screen verdict: no — it's a habit tracker, not a training log.

confusionPoints.what: App greeted me 'Morning, Noof' on a fresh install — no onboarding, never asked my name, weight, or goals. Felt like I was using someone else's account.

Goal was pre-filled at 158 lb with a '-1.0 lb/wk — my prescription' cut based on a '165.0 lb average' I never entered. I wanted to bulk to 205 and it refused: 'Cutting — target must be below your 165.0 lb average.' No gain/bulk option at all for a muscle-building user.
Could not find any way to close the sheet: no X, no Cancel, tapping outside did nothing, tapping the row again did nothing, switching tabs did nothing. I had to force-reload the app to escape.

Hesitated before tapping 'Done' — I expected it to open a set/rep entry flow. It just instantly logged the whole workout. The '1 lift' counter made me hunt for a lift-detail screen that doesn't exist.
Weight and body fat fields came pre-filled with 165 lb / 18% — values I never entered. Unclear if tapping Done would have silently logged those numbers for me.
Both features require adding my own API key in More ('costs about a cent a day'). As a new user I don't have an API key and wasn't going to get one mid-shake — so half the tab bar is dead on arrival.

issues.title: No way to dismiss the 'Set your goal' sheet
issues.screen: Set your goal sheet (opened from Today's Plan)
issues.what: The sheet has only 'Start plan' as an action. No close button, backdrop taps and tab-bar taps don't dismiss it. Only a full app reload got me out.
issues.expected: An X/Cancel button, or tap-outside / swipe-down to dismiss.

issues.title: Goal engine only supports cutting
issues.what: Entering a target above the stored average shows 'Cutting — target must be below your 165.0 lb average' and blocks the plan. There is no gain/bulk/maintain option.
issues.expected: Support a weight-gain target (or at least explain the app is cut-only) for users whose goal is building muscle.

issues.title: No exercise/set/rep/weight logging anywhere
issues.what: A workout is a single tap of a type (Push/Pull/etc.). The counter even says '1 lift', but there is no screen to record exercises, sets, reps, load, or RPE.
issues.expected: As an advanced lifter I expected at least optional set/rep/weight entry per session; without it the app can't replace or even supplement a training log.

issues.title: Fresh install is pre-loaded with someone else's identity and stats
issues.screen: Today screen, goal sheet, day editor
issues.what: After a clean start the app says 'Morning, Noof', claims a 165.0 lb weight average, and pre-fills Weight 165 / Body fat 18 in the day editor. There is no onboarding to set name, weight, units, or goal.
issues.expected: A first-run setup asking who I am and my current stats; empty fields until I log something.

issues.title: Food and Coach tabs require a bring-your-own API key
issues.what: Both tabs are placeholder cards saying to add an API key in More ('runs on your own API key, costs about a cent a day'). No fallback manual food entry on the Food tab itself.
issues.expected: Either features that work out of the box, or a manual food-log fallback that doesn't need a key.

issues.title: Day-editor sheet lingers in the page after Done and across tabs
issues.what: After tapping Done in the July 16 day editor, the editor's content (date heading, workout rows, Done button) remained present at the bottom of the page and was still there while browsing Food and Coach tabs.
issues.expected: The sheet should fully close and be removed once Done is tapped.

issues.title: First fill attempt on goal target hit a hidden duplicate input
issues.what: Two identical 'val' inputs exist, one invisible; programmatic fill landed in the hidden one and the visible field kept showing 158.0 until I targeted the visible input directly.
issues.expected: One input per visible field; stale hidden inputs removed from the DOM.

topImprovements: Add an optional detail layer on workout logging — even three fields (exercise, top-set weight, reps/RPE) after the one-tap 'Done' would make it usable for serious lifters without breaking the binary flow.
Add a real first-run onboarding (name, current weight, units, goal direction incl. bulk/maintain) and make every sheet dismissible with an X or tap-outside.
reports.verbatim: Prettiest habit tracker I've ever one-tapped, but it thinks my name is Noof, ordered me to cut to 158, and then trapped me in the sheet like a hostage negotiation. My bench day — 225 five-by-five, clean — went into this app as one green checkmark, so my Google Sheet has nothing to fear. Steps, sleep, and weight logging in one editor is genuinely slick; if I ever just want to be told I showed up, I know where to find it.

reports.missionNotes: Logged everything the app would accept: my 12-mile run (reduced to a single "Cardio — done" tap), sleep (Yes), eating (Yes — the real food logger is locked behind a bring-your-own API key), 27,000 steps, and my weight (after deleting a 165 lb / 18% entry the app invented for me). Set a "goal" — the sheet only offers weight or body fat, ignored the 128 I typed, and started a cut plan to its own default of 158. Verdict on cardio: it is a checkbox, literally — no distance, time, pace, or effort anywhere, and zero cardio presence in Trends. Home-screen decision: no — this is a lifter's body-comp tracker wearing someone else's name.

confusionPoints.what: Greeted with 'Morning, Noof' — I'm not Noof. No sign-up, no name prompt, no onboarding; it felt like I'd opened someone else's phone.
Only 'Target weight' and 'Target body fat' options. I'm training for a marathon — no race, pace, mileage, or any performance goal exists, so nothing fit me.

confusionPoints.where: Set your goal sheet after tapping Start plan
confusionPoints.what: Toast said 'Plan set' but the sheet stayed open and the number box showed 158.0 after I typed 128 — I couldn't tell what it actually saved (it saved 158).
'Runs on your own API key... Add API key in More' — I have no idea what API key I'm supposed to have or why logging food requires one.
A weight entry '165.0 lb · 18%' dated today existed before I ever entered anything — I couldn't tell where the app got a body that isn't mine.
No obvious place on Today to enter weight (found it buried in Trends via 'tap a day to edit'), and no place anywhere to enter run distance or time.

issues.title: Goal sheet silently discards typed target and saves its default
issues.what: I typed 128 into the target-weight box and tapped Start plan; Trends shows 'PLAN — CUT TO 158.0 LB', the sheet's prefilled default. No error or feedback that my number was dropped. (The steps field accepted typed input fine, so typing is inconsistent across fields.)
issues.expected: The plan uses the number I typed, or the field rejects my input visibly before saving.

issues.title: App fabricates a starting weight entry for a brand-new user
issues.what: With zero data entered, Trends showed a logged entry 'Jul 16 — 165.0 lb · 18%' and built a whole weekly cut table from it. After I corrected today's weight to my real 131, the plan card declared '0.0 lb to go · 34.0 lb ahead of plan' — celebratory nonsense.
issues.expected: A new user has no weight entries until they add one; the app should ask my current weight, not assume someone else's.

issues.title: Cardio is a one-tap checkbox with no data fields anywhere
issues.what: Tapping 'Cardio' instantly logged 'Cardio — done'. My 12-mile, ~96-minute marathon long run is stored identically to a 10-minute treadmill stroll: no distance, duration, pace, effort, or notes, and cardio never appears in Trends beyond a '+1 cardio' tally.
issues.expected: At minimum an optional distance/time field on a cardio log; ideally cardio volume visible in Trends/History.

issues.title: No onboarding; app is hardcoded to another person ('Noof')
issues.what: First launch greets 'Morning, Noof' with a preloaded Push/Pull/Legs split, a 5-session weekly quota, and a cut mindset already assumed. I was never asked my name, sport, or goals.
issues.expected: A first-run setup asking who I am and what I'm training for, or at least a way to set my name and week structure.

issues.title: Food logging feature requires user-supplied API key
issues.what: The tab's entire pitch ('Say what you ate once a day — Claude turns it into calories') is unusable out of the box: it demands I add my own API key in More, 'costs about a cent a day'. I don't have one. Only the binary Yes/No on Today worked for me.
issues.expected: Food logging works without me procuring developer credentials, or the tab clearly offers a manual fallback.

issues.title: Goal-type sheet doesn't close after Start plan
issues.what: After tapping 'Start plan' the toast confirmed but the sheet stayed open showing the untouched form; I had to tap outside to dismiss it.
issues.expected: Sheet closes on successful save, confirming what was saved.

issues.title: Weight entry point is buried
issues.what: Nothing on Today lets me log weight; I only found it by tapping an existing row under 'RECENT ENTRIES — TAP A DAY TO EDIT' in Trends.
issues.expected: A visible 'log weight' action on Today or an obvious add button in Trends.

issues.title: Content cropped at right edge of screen
issues.what: The 'Done' button, the Cardio chip, the workout-card 'More' button, and the checklist No buttons all render partially cut off past the right edge at my viewport width.
issues.expected: All controls fully visible without horizontal cropping.

issues.title: Plan landing date inconsistent between sheet and Trends
issues.screen: Set your goal sheet vs Trends
issues.what: The goal sheet says the plan 'lands Sep 3'; Trends says 'plan line lands Sep 4'.
issues.expected: Same date in both places.

topImprovements: Give cardio actual data: when I tap Cardio, offer optional distance/time/type, and surface cardio volume in Trends and History — right now a marathon block and a treadmill stroll are the same checkmark.

Fix first-run and goal setup: ask for name and current weight instead of shipping Noof's, honor the target the user types (and close the sheet on save), and add at least one performance-flavored goal alongside weight/body-fat.
reports.verbatim: It's a genuinely slick little habit tracker — dark, fast, one-tap everything — but it's built for a guy named Noof who lifts five times a week and wants to cut to 158, and it never once asked who I was. My 12-mile long run became the same green checkmark as a hotel-gym elliptical session, it invented a 165 lb body for me, and it ignored the goal I typed. Cardio is a checkbox here, so this doesn't make my home screen.

reports.missionNotes: Logged the full day through the simple paths: sleep = Yes (one tap), eat healthy = No (honest), 5,200 steps via the inline "enter count" box, tapped Cardio for the dog walk (counted as 1/5 for the week), and corrected the weight entry from a pre-existing 165.0 lb to 212. Tried to set a goal: typed 195 twice but the field snapped back to the prefilled 158.0 each time while a "Plan set" toast fired; after saving weight the header badge read "47.0 lb behind", suggesting the plan was built from the stranger's prefilled numbers. Refused the Food tab's Claude/API-key feature (do not have a key, did not understand or trust it) and used the Yes/No fallback instead. App stays on the home screen for now, on probation.

confusionPoints.what: Greeted as 'Morning, Noof' — never asked my name, name is not mine, and it was afternoon. Immediately felt like somebody else's account.
'Push' with a big green 'Done' button before I had done anything. As a beginner I do not know what Push/Pull/Legs/Recovery mean and nothing explains them.
Target weight pre-filled to 158.0 lb (I weigh 212) with '−1.0 lb/wk — my prescription'. Whose number? Whose prescription? I never entered any data.
Typed 195, hit Start plan, box reverted to 158.0 while a 'Plan set' toast showed. Did it save my number or not? Tried twice, same result, never got confirmation.
No visible close/X button and the sheet stayed open after 'Start plan'. Took about six taps (backdrop, title, finally the Today tab) before it was gone.

'Runs on your own API key, costs about a cent a day' — I do not know what an API key is, where to get one, why food logging costs money, or who 'Claude' is and where my meals get sent. Did not trust it, backed out.
A weigh-in for TODAY already existed (165 lb, 18% body fat) that I never entered. Could not tell where it came from — felt like someone else's scale was feeding my app.

confusionPoints.where: Today's Plan badge after saving weight
confusionPoints.what: '47.0 lb behind' — behind what? Day one and I am already failing a plan I never agreed to at a number I never chose.

issues.title: Typed goal weight does not save — plan silently uses prefilled value
issues.what: Typed 195 into the target-weight box and tapped Start plan, twice. Each time the box reverted to the prefilled 158.0 and a 'Plan set — milestones live in Trends' toast appeared. After logging real weight (212) the badge showed '47.0 lb behind', consistent with the plan being anchored to the prefilled 158/165 numbers rather than what I typed.
issues.expected: The number I typed is the number the plan uses, with the sheet reflecting it after saving.

issues.title: Sheets do not close after their primary action and have no close button
issues.screen: Set your goal sheet / weight sheet
issues.what: After tapping 'Start plan' the goal sheet stayed open; there is no X or Cancel. Tapping the backdrop and the page title did nothing I could see; it only went away after tapping the Today tab (about six attempts). The weight sheet likewise stayed open after Save.
issues.expected: Primary action saves and closes the sheet, or at least a visible Close control.
