issues.title: App ships full of a stranger's identity and data
issues.screen: Today header, goal sheet, weight sheet
issues.what: With zero setup the app greeted me as 'Noof', prefilled a 158.0 lb goal, and already had a weigh-in logged for today (165.0 lb, 18% body fat) that I never entered. I could not tell whose data this was or where it came from.
issues.expected: First run asks my name and starts empty; nothing pre-logged under today's date.

issues.title: Food logging headline feature demands a paid API key with unexplained jargon
issues.what: The main food feature says 'Runs on your own API key, costs about a cent a day' and sends what I ate to 'Claude'. No explanation of what an API key is, where to get one, what data leaves the phone, or who receives it. As a privacy-sensitive non-technical user I refused and backed out.
issues.expected: Plain-language explanation (or no key requirement) and a clear statement of what is sent where; the simple Yes/No fallback should be presented as a first-class option on this tab.

issues.title: Day-one guilt framing
issues.what: On my very first day the app said '5 to go, 4 days left — today counts', then 'under target' on my steps, then '47.0 lb behind' the moment I entered my real weight.
issues.expected: A new user's first day should establish a baseline, not declare them behind a schedule they never set.

issues.title: Beginner jargon unexplained (Push/Pull/Legs/Recovery)
issues.what: Workout types are single gym-slang words with no descriptions; 'NEXT UP: Push' with a prominent 'Done' button appears before any activity. I only logged 'Cardio' because walking seemed to fit.
issues.expected: A short description of each workout type, or a beginner mode; 'Done' should read as 'Mark Push done' so it is clear what tapping it claims.

issues.title: Greeting says 'Morning' regardless of time
issues.what: Screen said 'Morning, Noof' in the afternoon.
issues.expected: Time-appropriate greeting.

topImprovements: First-run setup: ask for the user's name and current weight, and start with zero pre-filled data — no stranger's greeting name, no prefilled 158 lb goal, no pre-logged weigh-in for today. Every prefilled number cost trust.

Make bottom sheets behave: the primary button (Start plan / Save) must commit the value actually typed, visibly confirm it, and close the sheet; add an X. Right now typed goal values silently revert to the default.
reports.verbatim: "It never asked me a single question but it already knew a name and a weight — neither of them mine. The Yes/No buttons are the easiest logging I've ever done, tap and 'noted,' I'll give it that. But it wants me to buy some 'API key' so a computer named Claude can grade my sandwich, and it told me I'm 47 pounds behind on day one — behind on a plan I never made."

reports.missionNotes: Logged a Push workout in exactly 1 tap: the Today screen opens with "NEXT UP: Push" and a giant Done button; tapping it flipped the weekly counter to 1/5 and showed "Push logged · tap to undo". Since it was fast, I poked the More tab (my privacy check): no account, no email, API key "stored only on this phone", and Export produced a local backup in one tap ("Last backup: 2026-07-16"). Verdict: it stays on my home screen.

confusionPoints.where: More tab, SYNC · SMART REMINDERS section
confusionPoints.what: A bare 'sync token' text box with no hint of what a sync token is, where to get one, or what server 'your private worker' is. As someone who guards his data I hesitated here — steps and weigh-ins 'logging themselves' implies something off-phone, but nothing says what leaves the device. I left it blank.
Greeting says 'Morning, Noof' and About says 'built for exactly one user' — took a beat to realize this app is hardcoded to someone else's name, not mine.

issues.title: Content clipped off the right edge of the screen
issues.what: In both screenshots, UI ran past the right edge: the Done button and 'Cardio' chip were cut off on Today (Cardio showed as 'C'), and 'sessions / week' / goal text were clipped on More.
issues.expected: Everything fits the screen width; no buttons half off-screen.

issues.title: 'sync token' field is unexplained
issues.what: An empty text box labeled 'sync token' with a Save button. No link, no explanation of where a token comes from or what data would leave the phone if I saved one.
issues.expected: One line telling me what it is and where my data goes, or hide the box until it's relevant. For a privacy-focused user an unexplained sync box is a trust wobble.

issues.title: 'Push logged · tap to undo' notice lingers across screens
issues.what: The undo toast from the workout log was still present on the More screen several actions and screens later.
issues.expected: Toast auto-dismisses after a few seconds instead of following me around the app.

issues.title: App is hardcoded to another user's name
issues.what: Header greets 'Noof' and About says 'NoofGains v1 — built for exactly one user.' I'm testing it as an outside user and there is no way to make it mine.
issues.expected: As a beta tester I understand it's a personal app, but if this is meant for anyone else, the name should be settable.

topImprovements: Explain the sync token where it's asked for — one sentence on what it is, where to get it, and exactly what data leaves the phone (or hide the field until a token exists). Fast loggers who are privacy-wary will never paste a mystery token.
Fix the horizontal layout so nothing clips off the right edge on narrow screens — the primary Done button and the Cardio chip were partially cut off.
reports.verbatim: One tap and my workout was logged with undo right there — that's faster than my rest timer, and I've never said that about an app. No account, no email, and backup is a file on my phone, which is the only acceptable answer. The mystery 'sync token' box gets a side-eye, but it's optional, so yes — it keeps its spot on my home screen.

reports.missionNotes: Found my answer on the Trends tab: 7-day avg 172.9, 2.9 lb to go, trending -0.8 lb/week, "1.7 lb behind plan" — the plan line lands Jul 24 but my pace says Aug 10, so the cut is working but ~2.5 weeks behind the app's schedule. The app's suggestion came from the weekly assessment note: "Week 6 missed (173.3 vs 171.7) and execution slipped — 4/5 sessions, 4/7 clean days. Same plan; run it before we touch calories." The Coach tab, where I expected personalized advice, required my own Anthropic API key so I couldn't use it. Logged today successfully: weight 172.5 (saved, plan badge updated live from 1.8 to 1.7 lb behind), sleep = yes, steps = 8,940 (auto-marked "target hit"). Also exported a backup after seeing a "Never backed up" warning.

confusionPoints.what: A red '1.8 lb behind' badge with no explanation on that screen — behind what? I had to go to Trends to learn it meant behind the plan line.

confusionPoints.where: Trends tab, plan card vs SIGNALS section
confusionPoints.what: Plan card says '1.7 lb behind plan' and 'your pace says Aug 10' (vs plan Jul 24), but SIGNALS on the same screen says 'Trending -0.8 lb/week (7-day avg). On pace.' I couldn't tell which verdict to believe.

W6 shows 173.3 and W7 shows 170.7 — a 2.6 lb drop expected in one week, which doesn't match the stated '1.1 lb/wk' plan rate. It looks like all my accumulated shortfall got dumped into the current week's target.
'Calories · protein 2,100 · 173g' has no label saying whether it's a target, an average, or yesterday's intake — it matches none of my logged food days (385–1,885 kcal).
Expected coaching advice; instead got a paragraph about API keys and a button sending me to a different tab (More) to paste a key from console.anthropic.com.
Greeting says 'Morning, Noof' but my profile name is Nora, and More has no profile/name setting to fix it. About says 'built for exactly one user' — made me wonder if I was looking at my own account.

issues.title: Trends contradicts itself on whether I'm on pace
issues.what: The plan card says '1.7 lb behind plan' with goal arrival 'your pace says Aug 10' vs plan line 'Jul 24', while the SIGNALS section on the same screen says 'Trending -0.8 lb/week (7-day avg). On pace.'
issues.expected: One consistent verdict on my progress — if I'm 1.7 lb behind a 1.1 lb/wk plan, the signals section shouldn't tell me I'm on pace. This is the single question a weekly check-in exists to answer.

issues.title: Coach tab unusable without a personal Anthropic API key
issues.what: The tab promises 'Reads everything you log. Ask it anything' but only shows an explainer and an 'Add API key in More' button pointing to console.anthropic.com. No advice is available without bringing my own key.
issues.expected: Some baseline guidance without developer-style setup, or at least the weekly assessment surfaced here. As a non-developer I have no API key, so the app's flagship advice feature is a dead end.

issues.title: Current-week milestone demands 2.6 lb loss in one week, contradicting the stated plan rate
issues.what: After missing week 6 (173.3 vs 171.7 target), the W7 box shows 170.7 as this week's target — a 2.6 lb drop in 7 days — while the caption still says '1.1 lb/wk' and the assessment note says 'Same plan; run it before we touch calories.'
issues.expected: A re-projected or rate-consistent milestone (e.g. ~172.2 at 1.1 lb/wk), or an explicit note that the deadline is fixed and the required rate has grown. As shown, the ladder tells me to crash-cut while the text tells me to change nothing.

issues.title: THIS WEEK 'Calories · protein' figure is unlabeled and doesn't match my logs
issues.what: Shows '2,100 · 173g' with no indication of whether it's a daily target, a weekly average, or actual intake. My logged food days ranged 385–1,885 kcal, so the number matches nothing I recognize.
issues.expected: A label like 'avg/day (3 logged days)' or 'target', and a number traceable to my own food log.

issues.title: Greeting ignores my profile name
issues.what: Header reads 'Morning, Noof' even though my profile name is Nora, and the More tab offers no profile/name setting. About section says the app is 'built for exactly one user.'
issues.expected: 'Morning, Nora' — the app clearly stores a name for me and should use it, or expose a setting to change it.

topImprovements: Unify the pace verdict: make Today's 'behind' badge, the plan card, and the SIGNALS line share one source of truth, and make the current-week milestone follow the stated lb/wk rate (or explain that the deadline is fixed) instead of silently compressing the whole shortfall into this week.
Give Coach a no-API-key mode: surface the weekly assessment and a concrete recommendation there directly, keeping the bring-your-own-key chat as an optional power feature.
reports.verbatim: "Logging is honestly the fastest I've used — weight, sleep, steps in under a minute, with undo toasts and the plan badge updating live. But my one check-in question, 'am I on track?', got three different answers on one screen: 1.7 lb behind, 'On pace', and a week-7 box quietly demanding 2.6 lb in seven days. Then the Coach tab asked me for an API key, which is where a normal person closes the app."

reports.missionNotes: Came back after 2.5 weeks away. The app made no comment on the absence — dashboard just said "NEXT UP: Pull" with a red "3.7 lb behind" stat. Logged weight 174.5 (worked, nice undo toast) and sleep (worked). The big green "Done" button for the workout did nothing after four attempts including an app reload; I finally logged the Pull session by going History > tap today > Log, after which the dashboard correctly showed "Pull — done", 1/5 ring, and eventually "All caught up". Verdict on lapse treatment: mostly welcoming (no shame copy, easy backfill, neutral "No weigh-ins in week 6 — the plan is blind without the scale"), but passively punishing in the numbers (impossible "5 to go, 4 days left" weekly target, "your pace says Jan 12" projection) and the Coach that might have said something human is locked behind an API key.

confusionPoints.what: Tapped the big green Done button twice (plus the Train — Pull checklist row, plus once more after reloading) and nothing happened — no toast, no ring change, no error. I genuinely wondered if I was tapping wrong.
Header says 'Morning, Noof' but my profile name is Sam. Who is Noof? Felt like I opened someone else's app.
Subtitle says 'First entry sets the baseline' even though I have a whole month of weigh-ins from June. Made me briefly worry my history was wiped.
'5 to go, 4 days left — today counts' — the weekly goal is already mathematically impossible on my first day back, so why am I being told today counts toward it?
It wants me to 'Add API key in More' and says it 'costs pennies per question'. I don't have an API key or know where to get one, so the whole tab is a dead end for me.
Shows 'Calories · protein 2,110 · 175g' and advises 'tighten the Tue/Thu dinners' — I have never logged a single meal, so I don't know where these numbers and my supposed dinner habits come from.

issues.title: Primary workout 'Done' button on Today dashboard silently does nothing
issues.what: Tapped the large green Done button on the NEXT UP: Pull card twice, tapped the 'Train — Pull' plan row, reloaded the app and tapped Done again — no toast, no ring change, NEXT UP never advanced, and no console error was produced. The only way to log the workout was History > tap today > Log next to Pull, which worked instantly and updated the dashboard.
issues.expected: One tap on the app's single biggest button logs the workout with the same immediate feedback the weigh-in gave me.

issues.title: Trends shows calorie/protein figures and meal-specific advice with zero food ever logged
issues.what: THIS WEEK panel shows 'Calories · protein 2,110 · 175g' with no label saying target or estimate, and SIGNALS says 'tighten the Tue/Thu dinners' — but my account has no food entries at all.
issues.expected: Either show a dash / 'no food logged' for calories, or clearly label it as a target; advice should not reference specific meals I never recorded.

issues.title: No acknowledgment of an 18-day absence anywhere in the app
issues.what: After 2.5 weeks away the dashboard resumes as if nothing happened: 'NEXT UP: Pull', red '3.7 lb behind', full 0/5 week. The only reference to the gap is a small line buried in Trends ('No weigh-ins in week 6 — the plan is blind without the scale'). No welcome-back, no offer to adjust or rebase the plan.
issues.expected: A brief, kind lapse-recovery moment: acknowledge the gap, offer to restart/rebase the plan, and frame today as a fresh start.

issues.title: Weekly training goal is already impossible on return day
issues.what: The Train row says '5 to go, 4 days left — today counts'. Five sessions cannot fit in four days, so my first day back opens with a goal I have already failed.
issues.expected: Cap or pro-rate the weekly target mid-week (e.g. 'aim for 4 this week') so a returning user starts with something winnable.

issues.title: Pace projection punishes the logging gap without context
issues.what: Plan card says 'plan line lands Jul 24 · your pace says Jan 12'. The January date comes from my 18-day no-data flatline and lands like a verdict, with no note that the estimate is skewed by the break.
issues.expected: Caveat the projection after a data gap ('skewed by 18 days without weigh-ins') or compute pace from active weeks only.

issues.title: Greeting uses 'Noof' instead of my profile name
issues.what: Header reads 'Morning, Noof' while my profile name is Sam.
issues.expected: 'Morning, Sam' — it's a personal app, use my name.

issues.title: Weigh-in card copy says 'First entry sets the baseline' for a user with a month of history
issues.what: After 27 weigh-ins in June, the morning weigh-in card still shows the first-run subtitle 'First entry sets the baseline'.
issues.expected: Copy that reflects my history, e.g. 'Last: 174.8 · Jun 27'.

issues.title: Content clipped at right edge of screen
issues.what: In my view the right side of the layout is cut off: the 'Cardio' chip, the 'More' button, and the red 'behind' stat are partially clipped off-screen (may be specific to this viewport width).
issues.expected: Cards and header stats fit fully within the viewport at narrow widths.

topImprovements: Fix the dashboard Done button — the app's core one-tap logging action fails silently, and on a comeback day that reads as 'even the app gave up on me'; the History backfill path proves the logging itself works.

Add a lapse-recovery moment for returning users: acknowledge the gap in one kind sentence, offer to rebase the plan/weekly target so day one back is winnable, and keep doom stats (impossible '5 to go, 4 days left', 'your pace says Jan 12') out of the first screen.
reports.verbatim: It was kinder to my ego than I expected — no dead streak, no guilt trip, and backfilling my day from History was painless. But the giant green Done button flat-out ignored me on the one day I really needed the win, and the app telling me to 'tighten the Tue/Thu dinners' when I've never logged a meal made me trust the rest of its numbers a little less.

reports.missionNotes: Ruth reviewed her six weeks across Today, Trends, History, Fuel, Food, Coach, and More. She successfully pieced together her story from the weight chart, body-fat chart, and calendar: roughly 178 down to 172.2 lb, body fat 24.1% to 22.6%, and 4-5 workouts every week. However, the app's own verdict confused her — "1.8 lb behind plan" and "Week 6 missed" in one place versus "Trending -0.8 lb/week. On pace." in another — so she could not confidently say whether the app thinks she is doing well. Along the way she accidentally triggered a "Pull logged" workout entry by pressing Done on a day-detail window, had to undo it, and fought the window across three tabs before it closed. She also discovered her dictated food diary is not viewable on the Food tab without an "API key."

confusionPoints.what: Greeting says 'Morning, Noof' — her name is Ruth; six weeks in she still doesn't know what a Noof is (About page later says 'built for exactly one user. LFG.').
Red '1.8 lb behind' with no explanation of what she is behind; only by digging into More did she learn a 'line' from 178 to 170 was started June 1.
'Cutting — down and to the right.' — 'cutting' is gym slang she half-knows; the phrase doesn't tell her if SHE is on track.
Week boxes W1 177.7 ... W7 170.7 are unexplained — targets or actual weights? Most are red (reads as failing every week) and W7 implies losing ~2.3 lb in one week from today's 173.0.
'1.1 lb/wk · plan line lands Jul 24 · your pace says Aug 11' directly contradicts the SIGNALS line 'Trending -0.8 lb/week (7-day avg). On pace.' — is she behind or on pace?
'Calories · protein 2,100 · 173g' sits among actuals (Sessions 1/5) but is actually the Fuel plan target — she thought the app knew what she ate this week.
'Slept well 1 / 5+ nights' — the '5+' notation was opaque; she read it as 'one out of five-plus nights?'
'Week 6 missed (173.3 vs 171.7) and execution slipped — 4/5 sessions, 4/7 clean days. Same plan; run it before we touch calories.' — 'execution', 'clean days', and 'we' are unexplained coach-speak.
'ghost-aligned so every shot matches' — no idea what ghost-aligned means.
Green line vs grey dots never labeled — she guessed line = 7-day average from the card title, dots = daily weigh-ins.
'WEEKS HITTING 5/WEEK' with axis '-7w ... -1w, now' — minus-week notation is programmer-speak, and only 2 of 8 squares are green despite training 4-5x every week, which reads as failure.

A full day's menu she never chose (office breakfast, Chipotle bowl, beef jerky) with '1,850 / 2,100 cal' progress-style numbers that read as food she already ate; 'Thu — Buy-lunch day' assumes someone else's life; P/F/C single-letter macros needed decoding.
Her two weeks of dictated food days are not shown — the tab only pitches 'Say what you ate... Runs on your own API key' with an 'Add API key in More' button; she thought her diary was lost.
'sync token', 'CLAUDE API KEY', 'console.anthropic.com', 'your private worker', '9pm push', 'iOS can evict web-app data', and 'LFG' — nearly every word in the setup sections was foreign.

issues.title: Pressing Done on the day-detail window logged a workout she never did
issues.what: After opening June 19 from the History calendar and pressing Done, a toast appeared: 'Pull logged · tap to undo' — the app recorded a Pull session for today, July 16. She only caught it because she noticed the small toast, and had to tap it to undo.
issues.expected: Done should only close the window. Nothing should ever silently add a workout to today's record from a button labeled Done.

issues.title: Day-detail window would not close and followed her across tabs
issues.screen: History day editor (June 19), persisting over Coach/History/Today
issues.what: The June 19 window stayed open after pressing its Done button twice, remained overlaid while switching to Coach and back to History, and only disappeared after tapping outside it. The 'Pull logged · tap to undo' toast also lingered the whole time.
issues.expected: One tap on Done closes the window immediately, on whatever tab.

issues.title: App contradicts itself about whether she is on track
issues.what: Plan card says '1.8 lb behind plan', 'Week 6 missed (173.3 vs 171.7)', and 'your pace says Aug 11' (vs plan Jul 24); the SIGNALS section on the same page says 'Trending -0.8 lb/week (7-day avg). On pace.'
issues.expected: One consistent, plain-English verdict: e.g. 'You're losing about 0.8 lb/week — a bit slower than your plan's 1.1, so the goal lands ~Aug 11 instead of Jul 24.'

issues.title: Logged food diary invisible without an API key
issues.what: Despite having six dictated food days in her data (the Trends page even displays a protein figure from them), the Food tab shows only an intro paragraph and an 'Add API key in More' button — no way to see her past food days.
issues.expected: Past logged days should be viewable regardless; the key should only be needed for logging NEW days via Claude.

issues.title: W1–W7 week chips are unexplained and mostly red
issues.what: A row of week boxes (W4 175.2, W5 174.2, W6 173.3 in red; W7 170.7 in green) with no label saying whether they are targets or her actual weights; red styling makes six faithful weeks look like six failures, and the strip is horizontally cut off (a stray '9' from a truncated earlier week shows at the edge).
issues.expected: A label like 'weekly targets', a clear marker of where she actually is, and no silent sideways cutoff.

issues.title: Fuel tab presents someone else's meal plan as if it were her day
issues.what: Preset menu ('OFFICE BREAKFAST', Chipotle double-chicken bowl, 'Thu — Buy-lunch day', beef jerky top-up) with '1,850 / 2,100 cal' and '153 / 173g protein' progress numbers that read as consumed food at 8am; nothing says it is a suggested template. Two food-related tabs (Fuel and Food) with unexplained different purposes.
issues.expected: Clear framing like 'Suggested menu for today' and a one-line explanation of Fuel vs Food.

issues.title: Consistency chart undercounts her effort and uses dev notation
issues.what: Only 2 of 8 squares lit green although the calendar above shows 4-5 workouts every week; axis labels are '-7w' through 'now'.
issues.expected: Show partial credit (4/5) rather than pass/fail squares, and human labels like 'Jun 1 wk'.

issues.title: App greets her with the developer's name
issues.what: Header reads 'Morning, Noof' although her profile name is Ruth; About says 'built for exactly one user. LFG.'
issues.expected: Greet the person by the name in the profile; spell out or drop 'LFG'.

issues.title: Setup sections are wall-to-wall jargon
issues.what: 'sync token', 'API key', 'console.anthropic.com', 'your private worker', '9pm push', 'iOS can evict web-app data', 'ghost-aligned', 'Cut/Bulk', 'clean days', 'P/F/C' — none defined in plain English anywhere.
issues.expected: A sentence of plain English per feature, e.g. 'A backup copy of your data in one file' style explanations.

topImprovements: Add a single plain-English progress sentence at the top of Trends that reconciles the numbers (e.g. 'Down 6 lb in 6 weeks at about 0.8 lb/week — slightly slower than your 1.1 lb/week plan, so 170 now looks like mid-August') and label the W1-W7 chips as weekly targets instead of unexplained red boxes.
Make the day-detail window's Done button only ever close the window (never log a workout), and let the Food tab show previously logged days without an API key.
reports.verbatim: I can see with my own eyes I've lost six pounds and never missed a week at the gym, but the app tells me I'm 'behind plan' in angry red on one line and 'on pace' two inches below it — which is it? Half the words in there — cutting, clean days, sync token, API key, LFG — mean nothing to me, and after six weeks it still calls me Noof. I pressed Done on a little window and it gave me credit for a workout I never did; if I hadn't spotted the tiny message, my honest little diary would have a lie in it.

reports.missionNotes: Found the Coach tab immediately from the bottom nav. It contains exactly one card: a pitch that the coach reads your last 90 days and "spots what's slipping," plus a single "Add API key in More" button — zero insights, zero questions, nothing without a bring-your-own Anthropic API key from console.anthropic.com. I don't have a key and wouldn't create one on a lunch break, so Coach was a dead end. The real coaching turned out to live in Trends: behind-plan callout (1.8 lb behind, plan lands Jul 24 vs my pace Aug 11), a weekly verdict ("Week 6 missed... Same plan; run it before we touch calories"), and a SIGNALS section — all free, all rule-based. So the app does give real advice without a key, just not in the tab named Coach.

confusionPoints.what: Header says 'Reads everything you log. Ask it anything.' but there is nothing to ask — no input, no sample insight, just an API-key gate. I expected at least a taste of analysis from data the app already has on-device.

confusionPoints.where: More screen (after tapping 'Add API key in More')
confusionPoints.what: The button dropped me at the top of the More page; the COACH · CLAUDE API KEY section is buried four cards down past Rotation, Weekly Goal, Goal, and Sync. Had to hunt for the thing the button promised.

Plan card says '1.8 lb behind plan' and 'your pace says Aug 11' (2.5 weeks late), while the SIGNALS section on the same screen says 'Trending -0.8 lb/week. On pace.' Two opposite verdicts about my pace — which one do I act on?
'Calories · protein: 2,100 · 173g' has no label saying whether it's an actual, an average, or a target. None of my logged days were 2,100 kcal, so I couldn't tell where the number came from.

issues.title: Coach tab offers zero value without a personal Anthropic API key
issues.what: The entire Coach tab is one explainer card and an 'Add API key in More' button. It advertises that it reads training, sleep, food, steps, weight and 'spots what's slipping,' but shows none of that without a key the user must generate at console.anthropic.com. Meanwhile the Trends tab proves the app can already produce a rule-based weekly verdict and signals locally, for free.
issues.expected: A free tier in Coach: surface the locally computed verdict/signals (the ones Trends already shows) with the AI Q&A as the key-gated upgrade — or at minimum one sample insight so the tab isn't dead weight for keyless users.

issues.title: Contradictory pace verdicts on the Trends screen
issues.what: Plan card: '1.8 lb behind plan', 'plan line lands Jul 24 · your pace says Aug 11'. SIGNALS section, same screen: 'Trending -0.8 lb/week (7-day avg). On pace.' The plan requires ~1.1 lb/wk, so -0.8 lb/wk cannot be on pace for the plan. No qualifier explains what 'on pace' is measured against.
issues.expected: One consistent verdict, or an explicit qualifier (e.g. 'healthy cut rate' vs 'behind YOUR plan'). Conflicting reads undermine trust in the app's headline advice.

issues.title: 'Add API key in More' button doesn't land on the key field
issues.what: Tapping the button navigates to the top of the More page. The API key section is the fifth card down; I had to scroll past Rotation, Weekly Goal, Goal, and Sync to find it.
issues.expected: The button should scroll to and focus the API key field, or the key input should live right on the Coach tab.

issues.title: THIS WEEK calories/protein stat is unlabeled and unverifiable
issues.what: 'Calories · protein: 2,100 · 173g' appears in the THIS WEEK panel with no indication of whether it's an actual, an average, or a target. The 2,100 figure didn't match any day I've logged.
issues.expected: Label the stat (e.g. 'avg of 3 logged days' or 'target') so I can trust it at a glance.

issues.title: Greeting ignores my profile name
issues.what: Header says 'Morning, Noof' even though my profile name is Kenji. The About section admits the app is 'built for exactly one user,' but as a tester with my own profile the hardcoded name reads as a bug.
issues.expected: Greet the name in the profile.

topImprovements: Give Coach a free tier: the rule-based weekly verdict and signals already shown in Trends belong in the Coach tab, with the AI Q&A as the key-gated upgrade — right now the tab named Coach is the only tab with no coaching in it.
Reconcile the pace verdicts ('1.8 lb behind plan' vs 'On pace') into one qualified statement, and label the THIS WEEK calories/protein figure so users know what they're reading.
reports.verbatim: The tab named Coach is a locked door asking me to go generate an API key at a dev console — on my lunch break, no thanks. The funny part is the app already coaches: Trends told me I'm 1.8 lb behind, landing Aug 11 instead of Jul 24, and to hold the plan before cutting calories — exactly what I wanted, just hidden in the wrong tab and contradicted two cards down by an 'On pace' signal.

reports.missionNotes: Went to More ("Setup & data") and pieced together where my data lives from three separate clues: the backup warning ("iOS can evict web-app data"), the Coach copy ("runs on your own API key, stored only on this phone"), and an empty sync-token field — conclusion: everything is on-device, nothing leaves unless I opt in. Pressed Export; the "⚠ Never backed up" line flipped to "Last backup: 2026-07-16" but I never saw where the file went. Logged tonight's sleep (Yes, labeled "Wed night · Jul 15") and steps (8,130 — "target hit"), and both survived a full reload. Trust verdict: cautiously keeping it — local-first with no account is exactly what I want, but the app made me deduce that instead of saying it.

confusionPoints.what: 'Your private worker' plus a sync-token field — whose worker, running where, receiving what? The one feature that implies data leaving the phone is the one with no explanation of where data goes. I don't have a token and don't know if I'm supposed to.
Nothing visibly happened except the small 'Last backup' line changing — no toast, no share sheet, no file path. I wasn't sure a file actually saved anywhere.

The app had rolled to Thursday Jul 16 while I was logging my Wednesday. Sleep was clearly labeled 'Wed night · Jul 15', but I couldn't tell which day my 8,130 steps got credited to — I meant Wednesday's steps.
'Morning, Noof' — my name is Dana. Only made sense after reading About ('built for exactly one user'), which raised its own question of whether this app is really meant for me.
My first tap on More didn't do anything — screen stayed on Today. Second attempt worked.

issues.title: No single plain-language statement of where health data lives
issues.what: To answer 'where does my data live?' I had to assemble evidence from three places: the backup warning implying browser storage, the Coach copy ('your own API key, stored only on this phone'), and an unexplained sync-token field. The sync section describes a 'private worker' without saying where it runs or what it would receive.
issues.expected: One sentence somewhere obvious: data stays on this device; nothing is sent anywhere unless you add a sync token or API key; export anytime. For a health app this is the deciding sentence.

issues.title: Data-loss warning and backup nudge only visible inside More
issues.what: The warning '⚠ Never backed up. iOS can evict web-app data — export a backup' told me six weeks of my data could be silently deleted by the OS, and I only saw it because I happened to open More tonight. Today screen showed no hint of it.
issues.expected: If the OS can evict my only copy of six weeks of health data, the never-backed-up state should surface on the main screen, not sit buried in a settings tab.

issues.title: Export gives no confirmation of what was saved or where
issues.what: Tapping Export produced no toast, dialog, or visible file destination; the only feedback was the caption flipping to 'Last backup: 2026-07-16'. The stamp updated even though I saw no evidence a file landed anywhere.
issues.expected: A confirmation with the file name and destination (or a share sheet), so the 'Last backup' stamp corresponds to a file I can actually point to.

issues.title: Midnight rollover makes late-night step logging ambiguous
issues.what: Logging just after midnight, the day had already rolled to Thursday Jul 16. The sleep prompt handled it well ('Wed night · Jul 15'), but the steps entry gave no date, so my Wednesday step count may have been filed under Thursday. The eat-healthy question for Wednesday was gone from Today entirely.
issues.expected: Late-night entries should be date-labeled like the sleep prompt is, or the app should ask which day I mean before the previous day's questions vanish.

issues.title: First tap on More tab did not register
issues.what: Tapped More once and the view stayed on Today; a second tap opened it.
issues.expected: Tab responds on first tap.

issues.title: Greeting uses someone else's name
issues.what: Header reads 'Morning, Noof' every time; my name is Dana and I found no screen showing or editing my own name. About says 'built for exactly one user', and apparently that user isn't me.
issues.expected: Greet me by my name, or don't use a name at all.

topImprovements: Put one plain-English privacy card at the top of More (and ideally first-run): 'Everything lives on this phone. Nothing is sent anywhere unless you add a sync token or API key. Export anytime.' — it would have settled in five seconds the question that kept me up tonight.
Make backups feel real: show what/where the export saved (file name, share sheet) and surface the 'never backed up / iOS can evict your data' warning on the Today screen instead of hiding it in More.
reports.verbatim: Honestly, this is the least leaky-feeling health app I've used — no account, no cloud, my own export file — but I had to play detective across three screens at midnight to confirm that, and 'iOS can evict web-app data' buried in a settings tab nearly gave me a heart attack. Say the quiet part out loud and I'm a keeper.

reports.missionNotes: Failed the core mission: there is no way to switch the app to bulk/gain mode. The goal editor (More > Edit goal / Set goal) rejects any target above the 7-day average with "Cutting — target must be below your 173.0 lb average" — tried 180 lb via Edit goal + Save, after End plan, and via a fresh Set goal sheet; all three rejected. In the process I ended my active cut plan (one tap, no confirmation) and was left with no plan at all. Calorie/macro targets never changed: Fuel still shows "Cut targets from your 173 lb average" (2,100 kcal / 173g P) even with no plan. Logging today worked great: weigh-in 172.2, Pull session, sleep/food/steps check-ins all saved in seconds.

confusionPoints.what: Went there first to change my plan; it's just an AI chat that demands an Anthropic API key from console.anthropic.com — nothing about goals, and useless without a developer key I don't have.
No cut/bulk/gain selector anywhere — only a target-weight number and 'Cutting — my prescription' text, so I had no idea how the app decides mode or how to tell it I want to gain.

confusionPoints.where: Edit goal sheet after tapping End plan
confusionPoints.what: The sheet stayed open showing the stale 'Cutting — target must be below your 173.0 lb average' message and rejected 180 again, even though the plan was just ended and the background said 'No goal set'.

confusionPoints.where: More > Set goal (fresh, no active plan)
confusionPoints.what: With zero active plan the new-goal sheet still enforced the cut-only rule and defaulted to 166 lb (another cut) — why is it 'Cutting' when I have no plan?
Header says 'Cut targets from your 173 lb average' even after my plan was ended — unclear where these targets come from or how to make them bulk targets.

issues.title: Impossible to set a muscle-gain (bulk) goal — goal engine is cut-only
issues.screen: More > Edit goal / Set goal sheet
issues.what: Any target above the 7-day average (tried 180 lb) is rejected with the toast 'Cutting — target must be below your 173.0 lb average'. Tried Save on the existing goal, Save after End plan, and Start plan on a fresh goal — all blocked. No mode toggle exists on Today, Coach, More, or either goal sheet.
issues.expected: Setting a target above my current weight should flip the plan to gain/bulk mode (e.g. +0.5 lb/wk pacing) or there should be an explicit cut/bulk/maintain selector.

issues.title: End plan is one tap, no confirmation, no undo — lost my plan trying to bulk
issues.what: Tapped 'End plan' hoping to unlock a gain target; the plan ended instantly with a 'Plan ended' toast (no confirm dialog, no undo offer). Since a gain goal is impossible, I'm now stuck with no plan at all and Today nags 'Set your goal'.
issues.expected: A confirmation ('End your cut? You'll lose the line started Jun 1') or an undo on the toast, like the weight-save toast has.

issues.title: Stale 'Cutting' validation after plan ends
issues.screen: More > Edit goal / Set goal sheet
issues.what: After End plan (and even on a brand-new Set goal sheet with no active plan), validation still says 'Cutting — target must be below your 173.0 lb average' and rejects higher targets.
issues.expected: With no active plan there is no 'cut' to enforce; a fresh goal should accept any sensible target and infer direction from it.

issues.title: Fuel targets don't react to plan changes — always cut targets
issues.what: After ending my plan, Fuel still shows '2,100 kcal · 173g P — Cut targets from your 173 lb average'. Nothing anywhere offers surplus/bulk nutrition targets.
issues.expected: Calorie/macro targets should follow my goal mode — surplus when bulking, and at least drop the 'Cut' label when no plan exists.

issues.title: Greets me as 'Noof' instead of my profile name
issues.what: Header says 'Morning, Noof' every time; my profile name is Omar. The About section says 'built for exactly one user', which explains it but feels broken as a tester.
issues.expected: Use the name from my profile.

issues.title: Coach feature requires bringing your own API key
issues.what: The whole tab is a pitch to paste an sk-ant-… key from console.anthropic.com; without one the tab does nothing.
issues.expected: A coach that works out of the box, or at least hide/soften the tab if it's unusable — as a normal user I have no idea what an API key is.

issues.title: New-goal sheet presumes another cut (defaults to 166 lb)
issues.what: After ending a cut at 172-173 lb, the fresh goal sheet pre-filled 166.0 lb with '−1.0 lb/wk — my prescription · ~7 wks'.
issues.expected: Ask what I want (cut/bulk/maintain) instead of prescribing a deeper cut by default.

topImprovements: Add a gain/bulk (and maintain) mode to the goal engine: a target above current average should start a surplus plan with +lb/wk pacing, and Fuel's calorie/macro targets should switch accordingly.
Make 'End plan' confirm or be undoable, and re-validate the open goal sheet after plan state changes so it stops enforcing a cut that no longer exists.
reports.verbatim: Logging is genuinely fast — weight, workout, check-ins, done in under a minute, love that part. But I finished my cut, tried to tell it I'm bulking to 180, and it flat-out refused: 'Cutting — target must be below your average.' I ended my plan trying to get around it and now I have no plan at all — an app that only lets you shrink is useless to me now.

reports.missionNotes: Logged today's weigh-in (172.5) from the Today screen — the weigh-in card is right at the top with the value pre-filled, which was genuinely easy — but the first Save press produced no visible response and it only saved on the second press. Found the weight trend on the Trends tab: "Trending -0.8 lb/week (7-day avg)", a clear 7-day-avg line chart from Jun 1 (~178 → ~172.5), and a Recent Entries list showing Jul 16 · 172.5 lb — all doctor-ready. Trust verdict: cautious pass — no account/sign-in, backup exports to a file I keep ("Last backup: 2026-07-16"), and the photo copy says "encrypted on this phone"; but the app greeted me as "Noof" (not my name), says it was "built for exactly one user," asks for a sync token and a Claude API key I don't have, and warns iOS can evict my data.

confusionPoints.what: It says 'Morning, Noof' — my name is Elaine. For a minute I wondered if I was looking at someone else's account, which for a privacy-nervous person is a bad first minute.

Pressed Save after typing 172.5 and nothing visibly happened — no checkmark, no message, 7-day avg unchanged. Didn't know if my weight saved until I pressed Save a second time and got the 'Weight saved' toast.
Top of page says '1.7 lb behind plan' in red, lower down it says 'Trending -0.8 lb/week. On pace.' Behind or on pace? I don't know which one to tell the doctor.

'sync token' and 'Claude API key (sk-ant-…)' fields with no explanation of what they are or whether the app works without them. I don't have these things. The app worked fine anyway, but the page never says that.

confusionPoints.where: Bottom of screen, several minutes after saving
confusionPoints.what: 'Weight saved · tap to undo' stayed on screen across three different tabs long after I saved. I was afraid tapping near it would undo my weigh-in.

issues.title: First tap of weigh-in Save gives no response; save only happened on second tap
issues.what: Typed 172.5 over the pre-filled 172.2 and tapped Save. Nothing changed: no confirmation, the weigh-in row stayed unchecked, panel stayed open, 7-day avg stayed 173.0. Tapped Save again and only then got 'Weight saved · tap to undo' and the row completed.
issues.expected: One tap saves and confirms immediately — or if the first tap failed, some visible sign of why. A person less stubborn than me walks away believing their weight was logged when it wasn't.

issues.title: 'Weight saved · tap to undo' toast never dismisses
issues.what: The undo toast from my weigh-in stayed pinned to the bottom of the screen for the entire rest of the session, across three tab changes and many minutes.
issues.expected: It should disappear after a few seconds. A stale always-present 'tap to undo' is an accidental-tap landmine that would silently delete my weigh-in.

issues.title: App greets me by the wrong name and says it was 'built for exactly one user'
issues.what: Header reads 'Morning, Noof'. About section reads 'NoofGains v1 — built for exactly one user. LFG.' I am not Noof.
issues.expected: Use the name from my profile, or no name. For a privacy-sensitive new user, being called someone else's name plus 'built for one user' reads as 'this app is not for you and maybe your data is in the wrong place.'

issues.title: Contradictory pace messaging on Trends
issues.what: Plan card: '1.7 lb behind plan' (red), 'your pace says Aug 10' vs plan's Jul 24. Signals section on the same page: 'Trending -0.8 lb/week (7-day avg). On pace.'
issues.expected: One consistent verdict on how I'm doing. I'm bringing this page to a doctor; I can't have it disagreeing with itself.

issues.title: More page assumes technical knowledge (sync token, API key, 'private worker')
issues.what: Two setup boxes ask for a 'sync token' and a 'Claude API key' with placeholder 'sk-ant-…', and copy mentions 'your private worker.' No plain-language line explaining what these are, that they're optional, or that everything works without them.
issues.expected: A sentence like 'Optional — the app works fully without this' and non-jargon labels. I ignored both fields and everything worked, but the page made me feel the app was half set up or fishing for something.

issues.title: Red-heavy 'behind plan' framing despite steady loss
issues.what: After losing ~5.5 lb in 6 weeks, the first thing on Trends is red: '1.7 lb behind plan', three red week-boxes (W4/W5/W6), and 'Week 6 missed... execution slipped.'
issues.expected: Someone tracking weight on doctor's orders needs the trend first (down 0.8/week!) and the scolding never. The negative framing nearly convinced me the news was bad when it wasn't.

topImprovements: Make the weigh-in Save respond on the FIRST tap — instant unmissable confirmation showing the saved number (and auto-dismiss the undo toast after a few seconds instead of leaving it on screen forever).

Give Trends one consistent verdict (reconcile 'behind plan' vs 'on pace'), and rewrite the More page in plain language: greet the user by their own profile name and mark sync token / API key as clearly optional with everything working without them.
reports.verbatim: I'll give it this much: the weigh-in box is right on the front page, the chart my doctor wants is one tap away, and it never asked me to make an account — my numbers stay on my phone in a file I can keep. But it calls me Noof, it made me press Save twice before believing me, and it wanted some 'API key' like I'm a computer programmer. I'll keep using it for the doctor — with a backup file, because it told me my data could vanish.
